revision.text:
'''金輪院'''（こんりんじ）は、[[大和郡山市]]小泉町にある[[天台宗]]の寺院。小泉庚申堂と呼ばれることの方が多い。山号は尭山山、本尊は秘仏「青面金剛夜叉尊絵像」。大和国の庚申信仰の総道場として知られる。
== 歴史 == 万治2年（[[1659年]]）、小泉藩第2代藩主片桐貞昌の家臣で茶人でもあった藤林直良（宗源）が創建し、小泉氏の祈願寺となった。明治7年から同9年まで教場として用いられた。
== 文化財 == *算額 明治13年（1880年） 安村清一郎・森内彌三郎奉納。本堂に掲額。
== 交通アクセス == [[JR大和小泉駅]]下車徒歩10分
[[Category:奈良県の寺|こんりんし]]

'''金輪院'''（こんりんじ）は、[[奈良県]][[大和郡山市]]小泉町にある[[天台宗]]の寺院。小泉庚申堂と呼ばれることの方が多い。山号は尭山山、本尊は秘仏「青面金剛夜叉尊絵像」。大和国の[[庚申信仰]]の総道場として知られる。
== 歴史 == 万治2年（[[1659年]]）、[[小泉藩]]第2代藩主片桐貞昌の家臣で茶人でもあった藤林直良（宗源）が創建し、小泉氏の祈願寺となった。明治7年から同9年まで教場として用いられた。
== 文化財 == *算額 明治13年（1880年） 安村清一郎・森内彌三郎奉納。本堂に掲額。
== 交通アクセス == [[大和小泉駅]]下車徒歩10分
[[Category:奈良県の寺|こんりんし]]

== 歴史 == 万治2年（[[1659年]]）、[[小泉藩]]第2代藩主[[片桐貞昌]]の家臣で茶人でもあった藤林直良（宗源）が創建し、小泉氏の祈願寺となった。明治7年から同9年まで教場として用いられた。
== 文化財 == *算額 明治13年（1880年） 安村清一郎・森内彌三郎奉納。本堂に掲額。
== 交通アクセス == [[大和小泉駅]]下車徒歩10分

'''金輪院'''（こんりんいん）は、[[奈良県]][[大和郡山市]]小泉町にある[[天台宗]]の寺院。小泉庚申堂と呼ばれることの方が多い。山号は尭山山、本尊は秘仏「青面金剛夜叉尊絵像」。大和国の[[庚申信仰]]の総道場として知られる。

{{Buddhism stub}}

'''金輪院'''（こんりんいん）は、[[奈良県]][[大和郡山市]]小泉町にある[[天台宗]]の寺院。小泉庚申堂と呼ばれることの方が多い。山号は尭山山、本尊は[[青面金剛]]。大和国の[[庚申信仰]]の総道場として知られる。
== 歴史 == 万治2年（[[1659年]]）、[[小泉藩]]第2代藩主[[片桐貞昌]]の家臣で茶人でもあった藤林直良（宗源）が創建し、小泉氏の祈願寺となった。 [[大徳寺]]玉舟和尚の筆による縁起「宗鐇記」によると、貞昌の父[[片桐貞隆]]が家蔵の什器を焼き捨てたとき灰の中から青面金剛像一体が発見され、この像が後に本尊になったという。 明治5年8月の学制に基づき、同6年12月23目に山本寿保邸を借用して開校されていた小学修道館が、同7年2月当院倉庫に移転して、同9年2月城行換邸に移転するまで教場となっていた。
== 文化財 == *[[算額]] 明治13年（[[1880年]]） 安村清一郎・森内彌三郎奉納。本堂に掲額。
== 交通アクセス == [[大和小泉駅]]下車徒歩10分
[[Category:奈良県の寺|こんりんいん]] [[Category:大和郡山市|こんりんいん]] [[Category:算額|こんりんいん]]
{{Buddhism stub}}

'''金輪院'''（こんりんいん）は、[[奈良県]][[大和郡山市]]小泉町にある[[天台宗]]の寺院。小泉庚申堂と呼称される方が多い。山号は尭山山、本尊は[[青面金剛]]。[[大和国]]の[[庚申信仰]]の総道場として知られる。
== 歴史 == 万治2年（[[1659年]]）、[[小泉藩]]第2代藩主[[片桐貞昌]]の家臣で茶人でもあった藤林直良（宗源）が創建し、小泉氏の祈願寺となった。
[[大徳寺]]玉舟和尚の筆による縁起「宗鐇記」によると、貞昌の父[[片桐貞隆]]が家蔵の什器を焼き捨てたとき灰の中から青面金剛像一体が発見され、この像が後に本尊になったという。
明治5年8月施行の[[学制]]に基づき、同6年12月23目に山本寿保邸を借用して開校されていた小学修道館が、同7年2月当院倉庫へ教場が臨時移転し、同9年2月城行換邸に移転するまでの間使われた。
== 文化財 == *[[算額]] 明治13年（[[1880年]]） 安村清一郎・森内彌三郎奉納。本堂に掲額。
== 交通アクセス == [[大和小泉駅]]下車徒歩10分
{{DEFAULTSORT:こんりんいん}} [[Category:奈良県の寺]] [[Category:大和郡山市]] [[Category:天台宗]] [[Category:算額]]
{{Buddhism-stub}}

== 歴史 == 万治2年（[[1659年]]）、[[小泉藩]]第2代藩主[[片桐貞昌]]の家臣で茶人でもあった藤林直良（宗源）が創建し、小泉氏の祈願寺となった。
[[大徳寺]]玉舟和尚の筆による縁起「宗鐇記」によると、貞昌の父[[片桐貞隆]]が家蔵の什器を焼き捨てたとき灰の中から青面金剛像一体が発見され、この像が後に本尊になったという。
明治5年8月施行の[[学制]]に基づき、同6年12月23目に山本寿保邸を借用して開校されていた小学修道館が、同7年2月当院倉庫へ教場が臨時移転し、同9年2月城行換邸に移転するまでの間使われた。
== 文化財 == *[[算額]] 明治13年（[[1880年]]） 安村清一郎・森内彌三郎奉納。本堂に掲額。
== 交通アクセス == [[大和小泉駅]]下車徒歩10分

[[大徳寺]]玉舟和尚の筆による縁起「宗鐇記」によると、貞昌の父[[片桐貞隆]]が家蔵の什器を焼き捨てたとき灰の中から青面金剛像一体が発見され、この像が後に本尊になったという。
明治5年8月施行の[[学制]]に基づき、同6年12月23目に山本寿保邸を借用して開校されていた小学修道館が、同7年2月当院倉庫へ教場が臨時移転し、同9年2月城行換邸に移転するまでの間使われた。
== 文化財 == *[[算額]] 明治13年（[[1880年]]） 安村清一郎・森内彌三郎奉納。本堂に掲額。

{{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|37|40|N|135|45|33|E|region:JP|display=title}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:こんりんいん}} [[Category:奈良県の寺]] [[Category:大和郡山市の歴史]] [[Category:大和郡山市の建築物]] [[Category:天台宗の寺院]] [[Category:算額]]

{{日本の寺院 |名称 = 金輪院 |画像 = [[ファイル:Koizumi-koshindo Konrinin Yamatokoriyama Nara pref Japan01n.jpg|300px|金輪院]] |所在地 = {{JPN}} 奈良県大和郡山市小泉町834 |位置 = |山号 = 尭山山 |宗旨 = |宗派 = 天台宗 |寺格 = |本尊 = 青面金剛 |創建年 = 万治2年（1659年） |開山 = |開基 = 藤林直良（宗源） |中興年 = |中興 = |正式名 = |別称 = 小泉庚申堂 |札所等 = |文化財 = 庭園、庫裏、表門、木造釈迦如来坐像（市指定） |公式HP = |公式HP名 = }} [[ファイル:Koizumi-koshindo Konrinin Yamatokoriyama Nara pref Japan02n.jpg|thumb|本堂]] '''金輪院'''（こんりんいん）は、[[奈良県]][[大和郡山市]]小泉町にある[[天台宗]]の寺院。
'''小泉庚申堂'''と呼称される方が多い。
山号は尭山山（ぎょうせんざん）、本尊は[[青面金剛]]。
[[大和国]]の[[庚申信仰]]の総道場として知られる。
== 歴史 == 万治2年（[[1659年]]）、[[小泉藩]]第2代藩主[[片桐貞昌]]の家臣で茶人でもあった藤林直良（宗源）が創建し、小泉氏の祈願寺となった。
[[大徳寺]]玉舟和尚の筆による縁起「宗鐇記」によると、貞昌の父[[片桐貞隆]]が家蔵の什器を焼き捨てたとき、灰の中から青面金剛像一体が発見され、この像が後に本尊になったという。
明治5年8月施行の[[学制]]に基づき、同6年12月23日に山本寿保邸を借用して開校されていた小学修道館が、同7年2月当院倉庫へ教場が臨時移転し、同9年2月城行換邸に移転するまでの間使われた。
== 文化財 == [[ファイル:Koizumi-koshindo Konrinin Yamatokoriyama Nara pref Japan09n.jpg|thumb|扁額]] *[[扁額]] - 明治13年（[[1880年]]） 安村清一郎・森内彌三郎奉納。本堂に掲額。
== 交通アクセス == *[[大和路線]] [[大和小泉駅]]下車徒歩10分
== 周辺 == *[[慈光院]] *[[松尾寺 (大和郡山市)|松尾寺]]
== 関連項目 == {{Commonscat|Konrin-in}} *[[庚申堂]]
== 外部リンク == *[http://www.city.yamatokoriyama.nara.jp/report/machikado/002232.html 青面金剛とくくり猿の庚申堂に行ってきました。] - 大和郡山市まちかどレポート134 *[http://www.city.yamatokoriyama.nara.jp/kouhou/tektek/002810.html 大和小泉の庚申さん～市長てくてく城下町] - 大和郡山市広報つながり 平成24年6月1日号 No.1026

{{日本の寺院 |名称 = 金輪院 |画像 = [[ファイル:Koizumi-koshindo Konrinin Yamatokoriyama Nara pref Japan01n.jpg|300px|金輪院]] |所在地 = {{JPN}} 奈良県大和郡山市小泉町834 |位置 = |山号 = 尭然山 |宗旨 = |宗派 = 天台宗 |寺格 = |本尊 = 青面金剛 |創建年 = 万治2年（1659年） |開山 = |開基 = [[藤林宗源|藤林直良（宗源）]] |中興年 = |中興 = |正式名 = |別称 = 小泉庚申堂 |札所等 = |文化財 = 庭園、庫裏、表門、木造釈迦如来坐像（市指定） |公式HP = |公式HP名 = }} [[ファイル:Koizumi-koshindo Konrinin Yamatokoriyama Nara pref Japan02n.jpg|thumb|本堂]] '''金輪院'''（こんりんいん）は、[[奈良県]][[大和郡山市]]小泉町にある[[天台宗]]の寺院。
'''小泉庚申堂'''と呼称される方が多い。
山号は尭然山（ぎょうねんざん）、本尊は[[青面金剛]]。
[[大和国]]の[[庚申信仰]]の総道場として知られる。
== 歴史 == 万治2年（[[1659年]]）、[[小泉藩]]第2代藩主[[片桐貞昌]]の家臣で茶人でもあった[[藤林宗源|藤林直良（宗源）]]が創建し、小泉氏の祈願寺となった。
[[大徳寺]]玉舟和尚の筆による縁起「宗鐇記」によると、貞昌の父[[片桐貞隆]]が家蔵の什器を焼き捨てたとき、灰の中から青面金剛像一体が発見され、この像が後に本尊になったという。
明治5年8月施行の[[学制]]に基づき、同6年12月23日に山本寿保邸を借用して開校されていた小学修道館が、同7年2月当院倉庫へ教場が臨時移転し、同9年2月城行換邸に移転するまでの間使われた。
== 文化財 == [[ファイル:Koizumi-koshindo Konrinin Yamatokoriyama Nara pref Japan09n.jpg|thumb|扁額]] *[[扁額]] - 明治13年（[[1880年]]） 安村清一郎・森内彌三郎奉納。本堂に掲額。
== 交通アクセス == *[[大和路線]] [[大和小泉駅]]下車徒歩10分
== 周辺 == *[[慈光院]] *[[松尾寺 (大和郡山市)|松尾寺]]
== 関連項目 == {{Commonscat|Konrin-in}} *[[庚申堂]]
== 外部リンク == *[http://www.city.yamatokoriyama.nara.jp/report/machikado/002232.html 青面金剛とくくり猿の庚申堂に行ってきました。] - 大和郡山市まちかどレポート134 *[http://www.city.yamatokoriyama.nara.jp/kouhou/tektek/002810.html 大和小泉の庚申さん～市長てくてく城下町] - 大和郡山市広報つながり 平成24年6月1日号 No.1026
{{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|37|40|N|135|45|33|E|region:JP|display=title}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:こんりんいん}} [[Category:奈良県の寺]] [[Category:大和郡山市の歴史]] [[Category:大和郡山市の建築物]] [[Category:天台宗の寺院]] [[Category:算額]] [[Category:Kōshin]] [[Category:Untranslated pages]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-金輪院-qq]] [[ja:金輪院]][[Category:Kōshin]] [[Category:Untranslated pages]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-金輪院-qq]] [[Category:Qq]] [[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]][[en:金輪院]][[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]]

'''小泉庚申堂'''と呼称される方が多い。
山号は尭然山（ぎょうねんざん）、本尊は[[青面金剛]]。
[[大和国]]の[[庚申信仰]]の総道場として知られる。
== 歴史 == 万治2年（[[1659年]]）、[[小泉藩]]第2代藩主[[片桐貞昌]]の家臣で茶人でもあった[[藤林宗源|藤林直良（宗源）]]が創建し、小泉氏の祈願寺となった。
[[大徳寺]]玉舟和尚の筆による縁起「宗鐇記」によると、貞昌の父[[片桐貞隆]]が家蔵の什器を焼き捨てたとき、灰の中から青面金剛像一体が発見され、この像が後に本尊になったという。
明治5年8月施行の[[学制]]に基づき、同6年12月23日に山本寿保邸を借用して開校されていた小学修道館が、同7年2月当院倉庫へ教場が臨時移転し、同9年2月城行換邸に移転するまでの間使われた。
== 文化財 == [[ファイル:Koizumi-koshindo Konrinin Yamatokoriyama Nara pref Japan09n.jpg|thumb|扁額]] *[[扁額]] - 明治13年（[[1880年]]） 安村清一郎・森内彌三郎奉納。本堂に掲額。
== 交通アクセス == *[[大和路線]] [[大和小泉駅]]下車徒歩10分
== 周辺 == *[[慈光院]] *[[松尾寺 (大和郡山市)|松尾寺]]
== 関連項目 == {{Commonscat|Konrin-in}} *[[庚申堂]]
== 外部リンク == *[http://www.city.yamatokoriyama.nara.jp/report/machikado/002232.html 青面金剛とくくり猿の庚申堂に行ってきました。] - 大和郡山市まちかどレポート134 *[http://www.city.yamatokoriyama.nara.jp/kouhou/tektek/002810.html 大和小泉の庚申さん～市長てくてく城下町] - 大和郡山市広報つながり 平成24年6月1日号 No.1026

revision.comment: Text replacement - "== 歴史 ==" to "== History =="
revision.text:
{{infobox religious building |name = 金輪院|native_name = 金輪院 |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image = [[ファイル:Koizumi-koshindo Konrinin Yamatokoriyama Nara pref Japan01n.jpg|300px|金輪院]] |address = {{JPN}} 奈良県大和郡山市小泉町834 |位置 = |山号 = 尭然山 |宗旨 = |宗派 = 天台宗 |寺格 = |本尊 = 青面金剛 |創建年 = 万治2年（1659年） |開山 = |開基 = [[藤林宗源|藤林直良（宗源）]] |中興年 = |中興 = |正式名 = |別称 = 小泉庚申堂 |札所等 = |文化財 = 庭園、庫裏、表門、木造釈迦如来坐像（市指定） |公式HP = |公式HP名 = }} [[ファイル:Koizumi-koshindo Konrinin Yamatokoriyama Nara pref Japan02n.jpg|thumb|本堂]] '''金輪院'''（こんりんいん）は、[[奈良県]][[大和郡山市]]小泉町にある[[天台宗]]の寺院。
'''小泉庚申堂'''と呼称される方が多い。
山号は尭然山（ぎょうねんざん）、本尊は[[青面金剛]]。
[[大和国]]の[[庚申信仰]]の総道場として知られる。
== History == 万治2年（[[1659年]]）、[[小泉藩]]第2代藩主[[片桐貞昌]]の家臣で茶人でもあった[[藤林宗源|藤林直良（宗源）]]が創建し、小泉氏の祈願寺となった。
[[大徳寺]]玉舟和尚の筆による縁起「宗鐇記」によると、貞昌の父[[片桐貞隆]]が家蔵の什器を焼き捨てたとき、灰の中から青面金剛像一体が発見され、この像が後に本尊になったという。
明治5年8月施行の[[学制]]に基づき、同6年12月23日に山本寿保邸を借用して開校されていた小学修道館が、同7年2月当院倉庫へ教場が臨時移転し、同9年2月城行換邸に移転するまでの間使われた。
== 文化財 == [[ファイル:Koizumi-koshindo Konrinin Yamatokoriyama Nara pref Japan09n.jpg|thumb|扁額]] *[[扁額]] - 明治13年（[[1880年]]） 安村清一郎・森内彌三郎奉納。本堂に掲額。
== 交通アクセス == *[[大和路線]] [[大和小泉駅]]下車徒歩10分
== 周辺 == *[[慈光院]] *[[松尾寺 (大和郡山市)|松尾寺]]
== 関連項目 == {{Commonscat|Konrin-in}} *[[庚申堂]]
== 外部リンク == *[http://www.city.yamatokoriyama.nara.jp/report/machikado/002232.html 青面金剛とくくり猿の庚申堂に行ってきました。] - 大和郡山市まちかどレポート134 *[http://www.city.yamatokoriyama.nara.jp/kouhou/tektek/002810.html 大和小泉の庚申さん～市長てくてく城下町] - 大和郡山市広報つながり 平成24年6月1日号 No.1026
{{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|37|40|N|135|45|33|E|region:JP|display=title}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:こんりんいん}} [[Category:奈良県の寺]] [[Category:大和郡山市の歴史]] [[Category:大和郡山市の建築物]] [[Category:天台宗の寺院]] [[Category:算額]] [[Category:Kōshin]] [[Category:Untranslated pages]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:金輪院}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-金輪院-qq]] [[ja:金輪院]][[Category:Kōshin]] [[Category:Untranslated pages]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:金輪院}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-金輪院-qq]] [[Category:Qq]] [[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]][[en:金輪院]][[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]] [[Category:Subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:qq]][[en:金輪院}][[Category:qq]] [[Category:new pages]][[sn:金輪院]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[sn:金輪院]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[sn:金輪院]][[Category:qq]][[sn:金輪院]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[sn:金輪院]][[Category:qq]][[sn:金輪院]][[Category:qq]][[sn:金輪院]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:金輪院]][[Category:qq]]

{{infobox religious building |name = 金輪院|native_name = 金輪院 |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image = [[ファイル:Koizumi-koshindo Konrinin Yamatokoriyama Nara pref Japan01n.jpg|300px|金輪院]] |address = {{JPN}} 奈良県大和郡山市小泉町834 |位置 = |山号 = 尭然山 |宗旨 = |宗派 = 天台宗 |寺格 = |本尊 = 青面金剛 |創建年 = 万治2年（1659年） |開山 = |開基 = [[藤林宗源|藤林直良（宗源）]] |中興年 = |中興 = |正式名 = |別称 = 小泉Kōshin-dō |札所等 = |文化財 = 庭園、庫裏、表門、木造釈迦如来坐像（市指定） |公式HP = |公式HP名 = }} [[ファイル:Koizumi-koshindo Konrinin Yamatokoriyama Nara pref Japan02n.jpg|thumb|本堂]] '''金輪院'''（こんりんいん）は、[[奈良県]][[大和郡山市]]小泉町にある[[天台宗]]の寺院。
'''小泉Kōshin-dō'''と呼称される方が多い。
山号は尭然山（ぎょうねんざん）、本尊は[[青面金剛]]。
[[大和国]]の[[庚申信仰]]の総道場として知られる。
== History == 万治2年（[[1659年]]）、[[小泉藩]]第2代藩主[[片桐貞昌]]の家臣で茶人でもあった[[藤林宗源|藤林直良（宗源）]]が創建し、小泉氏の祈願寺となった。
[[大徳寺]]玉舟和尚の筆による縁起「宗鐇記」によると、貞昌の父[[片桐貞隆]]が家蔵の什器を焼き捨てたとき、灰の中から青面金剛像一体が発見され、この像が後に本尊になったという。
明治5年8月施行の[[学制]]に基づき、同6年12月23日に山本寿保邸を借用して開校されていた小学修道館が、同7年2月当院倉庫へ教場が臨時移転し、同9年2月城行換邸に移転するまでの間使われた。
== 文化財 == [[ファイル:Koizumi-koshindo Konrinin Yamatokoriyama Nara pref Japan09n.jpg|thumb|扁額]] *[[扁額]] - 明治13年（[[1880年]]） 安村清一郎・森内彌三郎奉納。本堂に掲額。
== 交通アクセス == *[[大和路線]] [[大和小泉駅]]下車徒歩10分
== 周辺 == *[[慈光院]] *[[松尾寺 (大和郡山市)|松尾寺]]
== 関連項目 == {{Commonscat|Konrin-in}} *[[Kōshin-dō]]
== 外部リンク == *[http://www.city.yamatokoriyama.nara.jp/report/machikado/002232.html 青面金剛とくくり猿のKōshin-dōに行ってきました。] - 大和郡山市まちかどレポート134 *[http://www.city.yamatokoriyama.nara.jp/kouhou/tektek/002810.html 大和小泉の庚申さん～市長てくてく城下町] - 大和郡山市広報つながり 平成24年6月1日号 No.1026

{{infobox religious building |name = 金輪院|native_name = 金輪院 |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image = [[ファイル:Koizumi-koshindo Konrinin Yamatokoriyama Nara pref Japan01n.jpg|300px|金輪院]] |address = {{JPN}} 奈良県大和郡山市小泉町834 |位置 = |山号 = 尭然山 |宗旨 = |宗派 = 天台宗 |寺格 = |本尊 = 青面金剛 |創建年 = 万治2年（1659年） |開山 = |開基 = [[藤林宗源|藤林直良（宗源）]] |中興年 = |中興 = |正式名 = |別称 = 小泉Kōshin-dō |札所等 = |文化財 = 庭園、庫裏、表門、木造釈迦如来坐像（市指定） |公式HP = |公式HP名 = }} [[ファイル:Koizumi-koshindo Konrinin Yamatokoriyama Nara pref Japan02n.jpg|thumb|本堂]] '''金輪院'''（こんりんいん）は、[[奈良県]][[大和郡山市]]小泉町にある[[天台宗]]の寺院。
'''小泉Kōshin-dō'''と呼称される方が多い。
山号は尭然山（ぎょうねんざん）、本尊は[[青面金剛]]。
[[大和国]]の[[庚申信仰]]の総道場として知られる。
== History == 万治2年（[[1659年]]）、[[小泉藩]]第2代藩主[[片桐貞昌]]の家臣で茶人でもあった[[藤林宗源|藤林直良（宗源）]]が創建し、小泉氏の祈願寺となった。
[[大徳寺]]玉舟和尚の筆による縁起「宗鐇記」によると、貞昌の父[[片桐貞隆]]が家蔵の什器を焼き捨てたとき、灰の中から青面金剛像一体が発見され、この像が後に本尊になったという。
明治5年8月施行の[[学制]]に基づき、同6年12月23日に山本寿保邸を借用して開校されていた小学修道館が、同7年2月当院倉庫へ教場が臨時移転し、同9年2月城行換邸に移転するまでの間使われた。
== Cultural Properties == [[ファイル:Koizumi-koshindo Konrinin Yamatokoriyama Nara pref Japan09n.jpg|thumb|扁額]] *[[扁額]] - 明治13年（[[1880年]]） 安村清一郎・森内彌三郎奉納。本堂に掲額。
== 交通アクセス == *[[大和路線]] [[大和小泉駅]]下車徒歩10分
== 周辺 == *[[慈光院]] *[[松尾寺 (大和郡山市)|松尾寺]]
== 関連項目 == {{Commonscat|Konrin-in}} *[[Kōshin-dō]]
== External Links == *[http://www.city.yamatokoriyama.nara.jp/report/machikado/002232.html 青面金剛とくくり猿のKōshin-dōに行ってきました。] - 大和郡山市まちかどレポート134 *[http://www.city.yamatokoriyama.nara.jp/kouhou/tektek/002810.html 大和小泉の庚申さん～市長てくてく城下町] - 大和郡山市広報つながり 平成24年6月1日号 No.1026
{{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|37|40|N|135|45|33|E|region:JP|display=title}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:こんりんいん}} [[Category:奈良県の寺]] [[Category:大和郡山市の歴史]] [[Category:大和郡山市の建築物]] [[Category:天台宗の寺院]] [[Category:算額]] [[Category:Kōshin]] [[Category:Untranslated pages]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:金輪院}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] <!--金輪院-->[[category:qq close]] [[ja:金輪院]][[Category:Kōshin]] [[Category:Untranslated pages]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:金輪院}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] <!--金輪院-->[[category:qq close]] [[Category:Qq]] [[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]][[en:金輪院]][[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]] [[Category:Subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:qq]][[en:金輪院]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:new pages]][[sn:金輪院]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[sn:金輪院]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[sn:金輪院]][[Category:qq]][[sn:金輪院]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[sn:金輪院]][[Category:qq]][[sn:金輪院]][[Category:qq]][[sn:金輪院]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:金輪院]][[Category:qq]]

== 周辺 == *[[慈光院]] *[[松尾寺 (大和郡山市)|松尾寺]]
== 関連項目 == {{Commonscat|Konrin-in}} *[[Kōshin-dō]]
== External Links == *[http://www.city.yamatokoriyama.nara.jp/report/machikado/002232.html 青面金剛とくくり猿のKōshin-dōに行ってきました。] - 大和郡山市まちかどレポート134 *[http://www.city.yamatokoriyama.nara.jp/kouhou/tektek/002810.html 大和小泉の庚申さん～市長てくてく城下町] - 大和郡山市広報つながり 平成24年6月1日号 No.1026

[[大徳寺]]玉舟和尚の筆による縁起「宗鐇記」によると、貞昌の父[[片桐貞隆]]が家蔵の什器を焼き捨てたとき、灰の中から青面金剛像一体が発見され、この像が後に本尊になったという。
明治5年8月施行の[[学制]]に基づき、同6年12月23日に山本寿保邸を借用して開校されていた小学修道館が、同7年2月当院倉庫へ教場が臨時移転し、同9年2月城行換邸に移転するまでの間使われた。
== Cultural Properties == [[File:Koizumi-koshindo Konrinin Yamatokoriyama Nara pref Japan09n.jpg|thumb|扁額]] *[[扁額]] - 明治13年（[[1880年]]） 安村清一郎・森内彌三郎奉納。本堂に掲額。
== 交通アクセス == *[[大和路線]] [[大和小泉駅]]下車徒歩10分
== 周辺 == *[[慈光院]] *[[松尾寺 (大和郡山市)|松尾寺]]

{{infobox religious building |name = 金輪院|native_name = 金輪院 |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image = [[File:Koizumi-koshindo Konrinin Yamatokoriyama Nara pref Japan01n.jpg|300px|金輪院]] |address = {{JPN}} 奈良県大和郡山市小泉町834 |位置 = |山号 = 尭然山 |宗旨 = |宗派 = 天台宗 |寺格 = |本尊 = 青面金剛 |創建年 = 万治2年（1659年） |開山 = |開基 = [[藤林宗源|藤林直良（宗源）]] |中興年 = |中興 = |正式名 = |別称 = 小泉Kōshin-dō |札所等 = |文化財 = 庭園、庫裏、表門、木造釈迦如来坐像（市指定） |公式HP = |公式HP名 = }} [[File:Koizumi-koshindo Konrinin Yamatokoriyama Nara pref Japan02n.jpg|thumb|本堂]] '''Konrin-in''' (金輪院) is a temple of {{ill|Tendai Buddhism|ja|天台宗}} located in {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, {{ill|Yamatokoriyama|ja|大和郡山市}}, {{ill|Koizumi Town|ja|小泉町}}.
It is more commonly referred to as '''Koizumi Kōshin-dō'''.
Its mountain name is Gyōnen-zan (尭然山), and its principal image is {{ill|Aomen Kongo|ja|青面金剛}}.
It is known as the headquarters of the {{ill|Kōshin faith|ja|庚申信仰}} of {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}}.
== History == In Manji 2 ({{ill|1659|ja|1659年}}), a retainer and tea master of the 2nd lord of {{ill|Koizumi Domain|ja|小泉藩}}, {{ill|Sadamasa Katagiri|ja|片桐貞昌}}, established the temple, making it the wish-fulfilling temple (祈願寺) of the Koizumi family.
According to the origin legend "Sōryō-ki" written by the monk Gyokusen of {{ill|Daitoku-ji|ja|大徳寺}}, when Sadamasa's father, {{ill|Tadataka Katagiri|ja|片桐貞隆}}, burned and discarded the family's ritual implements, a single statue of {{ill|Aomen Kongo|ja|青面金剛}} was discovered amid the ashes; this statue later became the principal image.
Based on the Educational System enacted in Meiji 5, the elementary school Shūdōkan—which had been opened by borrowing the Yamamoto Jumpo Residence on December 23 of Meiji 6—was temporarily relocated to the temple's warehouse in February of Meiji 7, and remained in use until it was moved to the Jōkō Henge residence in February of Meiji 9.
== Cultural Properties == [[File:Koizumi-koshindo Konrinin Yamatokoriyama Nara pref Japan09n.jpg|thumb|Henga]] * {{ill|Henga|ja|扁額}} – Donated by Kiyoshiro Yasumura and Yasaburō Morinouchi in Meiji 13 (1880); hung in the main hall.
== Access == * On the {{ill|Yamato Line|ja|大和路線}}, it is a 10‑minute walk from {{ill|Yamato Koizumi Station|ja|大和小泉駅}}.
== Surroundings == * {{ill|Jikōin|ja|慈光院}} * {{ill|Matsuo-dera|ja|松尾寺 (大和郡山市)|lt=Matsuo Temple}}
== Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Konrin-in}} * {{ill|Kōshin-dō|ja|Kōshin-dō}}

{{infobox religious building |name = 金輪院|native_name = 金輪院 |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image = [[File:Koizumi-koshindo Konrinin Yamatokoriyama Nara pref Japan01n.jpg|300px|金輪院]] |address = {{JPN}} 奈良県大和郡山市小泉町834 |位置 = |山号 = 尭然山 |宗旨 = |宗派 = 天台宗 |寺格 = |本尊 = 青面金剛 |創建年 = 万治2年（1659年） |開山 = |開基 = [[藤林宗源|藤林直良（宗源）]] |中興年 = |中興 = |正式名 = |別称 = 小泉Kōshin-dō |札所等 = |文化財 = 庭園、庫裏、表門、木造釈迦如来坐像（市指定） |公式HP = |公式HP名 = }} [[File:Koizumi-koshindo Konrinin Yamatokoriyama Nara pref Japan02n.jpg|thumb|本堂]] '''Konrin-in''' (金輪院) is a temple of {{ill|Tendai Buddhism|ja|天台宗}} located in {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, {{ill|Yamatokoriyama|ja|大和郡山市}}, {{ill|Koizumi Town|ja|小泉町}}.
It is more commonly referred to as '''Koizumi Kōshin-dō'''.
Its mountain name is Gyōnen-zan (尭然山), and its principal image is {{ill|Aomen Kongo|ja|青面金剛}}.
It is known as the headquarters of the {{ill|Kōshin faith|ja|庚申信仰}} of {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}}.
== History == In Manji 2 ({{ill|1659|ja|1659年}}), a retainer and tea master of the 2nd lord of {{ill|Koizumi Domain|ja|小泉藩}}, {{ill|Sadamasa Katagiri|ja|片桐貞昌}}, established the temple, making it the wish-fulfilling temple (祈願寺) of the Koizumi family.
According to the origin legend "Sōryō-ki" written by the monk Gyokusen of {{ill|Daitoku-ji|ja|大徳寺}}, when Sadamasa's father, {{ill|Tadataka Katagiri|ja|片桐貞隆}}, burned and discarded the family's ritual implements, a single statue of {{ill|Aomen Kongo|ja|青面金剛}} was discovered amid the ashes; this statue later became the principal image.
Based on the Educational System enacted in Meiji 5, the elementary school Shūdōkan—which had been opened by borrowing the Yamamoto Jumpo Residence on December 23 of Meiji 6—was temporarily relocated to the temple's warehouse in February of Meiji 7, and remained in use until it was moved to the Jōkō Henge residence in February of Meiji 9.
== Cultural Properties == [[File:Koizumi-koshindo Konrinin Yamatokoriyama Nara pref Japan09n.jpg|thumb|Henga]] * {{ill|Henga|ja|扁額}} – Donated by Kiyoshiro Yasumura and Yasaburō Morinouchi in Meiji 13 (1880); hung in the main hall.
== Access == * On the {{ill|Yamato Line|ja|大和路線}}, it is a 10‑minute walk from {{ill|Yamato Koizumi Station|ja|大和小泉駅}}.
== Surroundings == * {{ill|Jikōin|ja|慈光院}} * {{ill|Matsuo-dera|ja|松尾寺 (大和郡山市)|lt=Matsuo Temple}}
== Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Konrin-in}} * {{ill|Kōshin-dō|ja|Kōshin-dō}}
== External Links == * [http://www.city.yamatokoriyama.nara.jp/report/machikado/002232.html I visited the Kōshin-dō of Aomen Kongo and Kukuri Saru] – Yamatokoriyama City Machikado Report 134 * [http://www.city.yamatokoriyama.nara.jp/kouhou/tektek/002810.html The Kōshin of Yamato Koizumi – A Mayor’s Walking Tour of the Castle Town] – Yamatokoriyama City Public Relations Tsunagari, Issue No.1026, June 1, Heisei 24

{{infobox religious building |name = 金輪院|native_name = 金輪院 |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image = [[File:Koizumi-koshindo Konrinin Yamatokoriyama Nara pref Japan01n.jpg|300px|金輪院]] |address = {{JPN}} 奈良県大和郡山市小泉町834 |位置 = |山号 = 尭然山 |宗旨 = |宗派 = 天台宗 |寺格 = |本尊 = 青面金剛 |創建年 = 万治2年（1659年） |開山 = |開基 = [[藤林宗源|藤林直良（宗源）]] |中興年 = |中興 = |正式名 = |別称 = 小泉Kōshin-dō |札所等 = |文化財 = 庭園、庫裏、表門、木造釈迦如来坐像（市指定） |公式HP = |公式HP名 = }} [[File:Koizumi-koshindo Konrinin Yamatokoriyama Nara pref Japan02n.jpg|thumb|本堂]] '''Konrin-in''' (金輪院) is a temple of {{ill|Tendai Buddhism|ja|天台宗}} located in {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, {{ill|Yamatokoriyama|ja|大和郡山市}}, {{ill|Koizumi Town|ja|小泉町}}.
It is more commonly referred to as '''Koizumi Kōshin-dō'''.
Its mountain name is Gyōnen-zan (尭然山), and its principal image is {{ill|Aomen Kongo|ja|青面金剛}}.
It is known as the headquarters of the {{ill|Kōshin faith|ja|庚申信仰}} of {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}}.
== History == In Manji 2 ({{ill|1659|ja|1659年}}), a retainer and tea master of the 2nd lord of {{ill|Koizumi Domain|ja|小泉藩}}, {{ill|Sadamasa Katagiri|ja|片桐貞昌}}, established the temple, making it the wish-fulfilling temple (祈願寺) of the Koizumi family.
According to the origin legend "Sōryō-ki" written by the monk Gyokusen of {{ill|Daitoku-ji|ja|大徳寺}}, when Sadamasa's father, {{ill|Tadataka Katagiri|ja|片桐貞隆}}, burned and discarded the family's ritual implements, a single statue of {{ill|Aomen Kongo|ja|青面金剛}} was discovered amid the ashes; this statue later became the principal image.
Based on the Educational System enacted in Meiji 5, the elementary school Shūdōkan—which had been opened by borrowing the Yamamoto Jumpo Residence on December 23 of Meiji 6—was temporarily relocated to the temple's warehouse in February of Meiji 7, and remained in use until it was moved to the Jōkō Henge residence in February of Meiji 9.
== Cultural Properties == [[File:Koizumi-koshindo Konrinin Yamatokoriyama Nara pref Japan09n.jpg|thumb|Henga]] * {{ill|Henga|ja|扁額}} – Donated by Kiyoshiro Yasumura and Yasaburō Morinouchi in Meiji 13 (1880); hung in the main hall.
== Access == * On the {{ill|Yamato Line|ja|大和路線}}, it is a 10‑minute walk from {{ill|Yamato Koizumi Station|ja|大和小泉駅}}.
== Surroundings == * {{ill|Jikōin|ja|慈光院}} * {{ill|Matsuo-dera|ja|松尾寺 (大和郡山市)|lt=Matsuo Temple}}
== Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Konrin-in}} * {{ill|Kōshin-dō|ja|Kōshin-dō}}

{{infobox religious building |name = 金輪院|native_name = 金輪院 |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image = [[File:Koizumi-koshindo Konrinin Yamatokoriyama Nara pref Japan01n.jpg|300px|金輪院]] |address = {{JPN}} 奈良県大和郡山市小泉町834 |位置 = |山号 = 尭然山 |宗旨 = |宗派 = 天台宗 |寺格 = |本尊 = 青面金剛 |創建年 = 万治2年（1659年） |開山 = |開基 = [[藤林宗源|藤林直良（宗源）]] |中興年 = |中興 = |正式名 = |別称 = 小泉Kōshin-dō |札所等 = |文化財 = 庭園、庫裏、表門、木造釈迦如来坐像（市指定） |公式HP = |公式HP名 = }} [[File:Koizumi-koshindo Konrinin Yamatokoriyama Nara pref Japan02n.jpg|thumb|本堂]] '''Konrin-in''' (金輪院) is a temple of {{ill|Tendai Buddhism|ja|天台宗}} located in {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, {{ill|Yamatokoriyama|ja|大和郡山市}}, {{ill|Koizumi Town|ja|小泉町}}.
It is more commonly referred to as '''Koizumi Kōshin-dō'''.
Its mountain name is Gyōnen-zan (尭然山), and its principal image is {{ill|Aomen Kongo|ja|青面金剛}}.
It is known as the headquarters of the {{ill|Kōshin faith|ja|庚申信仰}} of {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }}.
== History == In Manji 2 ({{ill|1659|ja|1659年}}), a retainer and tea master of the 2nd lord of {{ill|Koizumi Domain|ja|小泉藩}}, {{ill|Sadamasa Katagiri|ja|片桐貞昌}}, established the temple, making it the wish-fulfilling temple (祈願寺) of the Koizumi family.
According to the origin legend "Sōryō-ki" written by the monk Gyokusen of {{ill|Daitoku-ji|ja|大徳寺}}, when Sadamasa's father, {{ill|Tadataka Katagiri|ja|片桐貞隆}}, burned and discarded the family's ritual implements, a single statue of {{ill|Aomen Kongo|ja|青面金剛}} was discovered amid the ashes; this statue later became the principal image.
Based on the Educational System enacted in Meiji 5, the elementary school Shūdōkan—which had been opened by borrowing the Yamamoto Jumpo Residence on December 23 of Meiji 6—was temporarily relocated to the temple's warehouse in February of Meiji 7, and remained in use until it was moved to the Jōkō Henge residence in February of Meiji 9.
== Cultural Properties == [[File:Koizumi-koshindo Konrinin Yamatokoriyama Nara pref Japan09n.jpg|thumb|Henga]] * {{ill|Henga|ja|扁額}} – Donated by Kiyoshiro Yasumura and Yasaburō Morinouchi in Meiji 13 (1880); hung in the main hall.
== Access == * On the {{ill|Yamato Line|ja|大和路線}}, it is a 10‑minute walk from {{ill|Yamato Koizumi Station|ja|大和小泉駅}}.
== Surroundings == * {{ill|Jikōin|ja|慈光院}} * {{ill|Matsuo-dera|ja|松尾寺 (大和郡山市)|lt=Matsuo Temple}}
== Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Konrin-in}} * {{ill|Kōshin-dō|ja|Kōshin-dō}}

{{infobox religious building |name = 金輪院|native_name = 金輪院 |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image = [[File:Koizumi-koshindo Konrinin Yamatokoriyama Nara pref Japan01n.jpg|300px|金輪院]] |address = {{JPN}} 奈良県大和郡山市小泉町834 |位置 = |山号 = 尭然山 |宗旨 = |宗派 = 天台宗 |寺格 = |本尊 = 青面金剛 |創建年 = 万治2年（1659年） |開山 = |開基 = [[藤林宗源|藤林直良（宗源）]] |中興年 = |中興 = |正式名 = |別称 = 小泉Kōshin-dō |札所等 = |文化財 = 庭園、庫裏、表門、木造釈迦如来坐像（市指定） |公式HP = |公式HP名 = }} [[File:Koizumi-koshindo Konrinin Yamatokoriyama Nara pref Japan02n.jpg|thumb|本堂]] '''Konrin-in''' (金輪院) is a temple of {{ill|Tendai Buddhism|ja|天台宗}} located in {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, {{ill|Yamatokoriyama|ja|大和郡山市}}, {{ill|Koizumi Town|ja|小泉町}}.
It is more commonly referred to as '''Koizumi Kōshin-dō'''.
Its mountain name is Gyōnen-zan (尭然山), and its principal image is {{ill|Aomen Kongo|ja|青面金剛}}.
It is known as the headquarters of the {{ill|Kōshin faith|ja|庚申信仰}} of {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }}.
== History == In Manji 2 ({{ill|1659|ja|1659年}}), a retainer and tea master of the 2nd lord of {{ill|Koizumi Domain|ja|小泉藩}}, {{ill|Sadamasa Katagiri|ja|片桐貞昌}}, established the temple, making it the wish-fulfilling temple (祈願寺) of the Koizumi family.
According to the origin legend "Sōryō-ki" written by the monk Gyokusen of {{ill|Daitoku-ji|ja|大徳寺}}, when Sadamasa's father, {{ill|Tadataka Katagiri|ja|片桐貞隆}}, burned and discarded the family's ritual implements, a single statue of {{ill|Aomen Kongo|ja|青面金剛}} was discovered amid the ashes; this statue later became the principal image.
Based on the Educational System enacted in Meiji 5, the elementary school Shūdōkan—which had been opened by borrowing the Yamamoto Jumpo Residence on December 23 of Meiji 6—was temporarily relocated to the temple's warehouse in February of Meiji 7, and remained in use until it was moved to the Jōkō Henge residence in February of Meiji 9.
== Cultural Properties == [[File:Koizumi-koshindo Konrinin Yamatokoriyama Nara pref Japan09n.jpg|thumb|Henga]] * {{ill|Henga|ja|扁額}} – Donated by Kiyoshiro Yasumura and Yasaburō Morinouchi in Meiji 13 (1880); hung in the main hall.
== Access == * On the {{ill|Yamato Line|ja|大和路線}}, it is a 10‑minute walk from {{ill|Yamato Koizumi Station|ja|大和小泉駅}}.
== Surroundings == * {{ill|Jikōin|ja|慈光院}} * {{ill|Matsuo-dera|ja|松尾寺 (大和郡山市)|lt=Matsuo Temple}}
== Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Konrin-in}} * {{ill|Kōshin-dō|ja|Kōshin-dō}}

{{infobox religious building |name = 金輪院|native_name = 金輪院 |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image = [[File:Koizumi-koshindo Konrinin Yamatokoriyama Nara pref Japan01n.jpg|300px|金輪院]] |address = {{JPN}} 奈良県大和郡山市小泉町834 |位置 = |山号 = 尭然山 |宗旨 = |宗派 = 天台宗 |寺格 = |本尊 = 青面金剛 |創建年 = 万治2nd year （1659th year ） |開山 = |開基 = [[藤林宗源|藤林直良（宗源）]] |中興年 = |中興 = |正式名 = |別称 = 小泉Kōshin-dō |札所等 = |文化財 = 庭園、庫裏、表門、木造釈迦如来坐像（市指定） |公式HP = |公式HP名 = }} [[File:Koizumi-koshindo Konrinin Yamatokoriyama Nara pref Japan02n.jpg|thumb|本堂]] '''Konrin-in''' (金輪院) is a temple of {{ill|Tendai Buddhism|ja|天台宗}} located in {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, {{ill|Yamatokoriyama|ja|大和郡山市}}, {{ill|Koizumi Town|ja|小泉町}}.
It is more commonly referred to as '''Koizumi Kōshin-dō'''.
Its mountain name is Gyōnen-zan (尭然山), and its principal image is {{ill|Aomen Kongo|ja|青面金剛}}.
It is known as the headquarters of the {{ill|Kōshin faith|ja|庚申信仰}} of {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }}.
== History == In Manji 2 ({{ill|1659|ja|1659th year }}), a retainer and tea master of the 2nd lord of {{ill|Koizumi Domain|ja|小泉藩}}, {{ill|Sadamasa Katagiri|ja|片桐貞昌}}, established the temple, making it the wish-fulfilling temple (祈願寺) of the Koizumi family.
According to the origin legend "Sōryō-ki" written by the monk Gyokusen of {{ill|Daitoku-ji|ja|大徳寺}}, when Sadamasa's father, {{ill|Tadataka Katagiri|ja|片桐貞隆}}, burned and discarded the family's ritual implements, a single statue of {{ill|Aomen Kongo|ja|青面金剛}} was discovered amid the ashes; this statue later became the principal image.
Based on the Educational System enacted in Meiji 5, the elementary school Shūdōkan—which had been opened by borrowing the Yamamoto Jumpo Residence on December 23 of Meiji 6—was temporarily relocated to the temple's warehouse in February of Meiji 7, and remained in use until it was moved to the Jōkō Henge residence in February of Meiji 9.
== Cultural Properties == [[File:Koizumi-koshindo Konrinin Yamatokoriyama Nara pref Japan09n.jpg|thumb|Henga]] * {{ill|Henga|ja|扁額}} – Donated by Kiyoshiro Yasumura and Yasaburō Morinouchi in Meiji 13 (1880); hung in the main hall.
== Access == * On the {{ill|Yamato Line|ja|大和路線}}, it is a 10‑minute walk from {{ill|Yamato Koizumi Station|ja|大和小泉駅}}.
== Surroundings == * {{ill|Jikōin|ja|慈光院}} * {{ill|Matsuo-dera|ja|松尾寺 (大和郡山市)|lt=Matsuo Temple}}
== Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Konrin-in}} * {{ill|Kōshin-dō|ja|Kōshin-dō}}

== Cultural Properties == [[File:Koizumi-koshindo Konrinin Yamatokoriyama Nara pref Japan09n.jpg|thumb|Henga]] * {{ill|Henga|ja|扁額|12=draft}} – Donated by Kiyoshiro Yasumura and Yasaburō Morinouchi in Meiji 13 (1880); hung in the main hall.
== Access == * On the {{ill|Yamato Line|ja|大和路線|12=draft}}, it is a 10‑minute walk from {{ill|Yamato Koizumi Station|ja|大和小泉駅|12=draft}}.
== Surroundings == * {{ill|Jikōin|ja|慈光院|12=draft}} * {{ill|Matsuo-dera|ja|松尾寺 (大和郡山市)|lt=Matsuo Temple|12=draft}}
== Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Konrin-in}} * {{ill|Kōshin-dō|ja|Kōshin-dō|12=draft}}

{{infobox religious building |name = 金輪院|native_name = 金輪院 |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image = [[File:Koizumi-koshindo Konrinin Yamatokoriyama Nara pref Japan01n.jpg|300px|金輪院]] |address = {{JPN}} 奈良県大和郡山市小泉町834 |位置 = |山号 = 尭然山 |宗旨 = |宗派 = 天台宗 |寺格 = |本尊 = 青面金剛 |創建年 = 万治2nd year （1659th year ） |開山 = |開基 = [[藤林宗源|藤林直良（宗源）]] |中興年 = |中興 = |正式名 = |別称 = 小泉Kōshin-dō |札所等 = |文化財 = 庭園、庫裏、表門、木造釈迦如来坐像（市指定） |公式HP = |公式HP名 = }} [[File:Koizumi-koshindo Konrinin Yamatokoriyama Nara pref Japan02n.jpg|thumb|本堂]] '''Konrin-in''' (金輪院) is a temple of {{ill|Tendai Buddhism|ja|天台宗|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yamatokoriyama|ja|大和郡山市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Koizumi Town|ja|小泉町|12=draft}}.

Its mountain name is Gyōnen-zan (尭然山), and its principal image is {{ill|Aomen Kongo|ja|青面金剛|12=draft}}.
It is known as the headquarters of the {{ill|Kōshin faith|ja|庚申信仰|12=draft}} of {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province|12=draft}}.

According to the origin legend "Sōryō-ki" written by the monk Gyokusen of {{ill|Daitoku-ji|ja|大徳寺|12=draft}}, when Sadamasa's father, {{ill|Tadataka Katagiri|ja|片桐貞隆|12=draft}}, burned and discarded the family's ritual implements, a single statue of {{ill|Aomen Kongo|ja|青面金剛|12=draft}} was discovered amid the ashes; this statue later became the principal image.
Based on the Educational System enacted in Meiji 5, the elementary school Shūdōkan—which had been opened by borrowing the Yamamoto Jumpo Residence on December 23 of Meiji 6—was temporarily relocated to the temple's warehouse in February of Meiji 7, and remained in use until it was moved to the Jōkō Henge residence in February of Meiji 9.

== Access == * On the {{ill|Yamato Line|ja|大和路線|12=draft}}, it is a 10‑minute walk from {{ill|Yamato Koizumi Station|ja|大和小泉駅|12=draft}}.

27 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:金輪院 up to rev 95816798
revision.text:
{{日本の寺院 |名称 = 金輪院 |画像 = [[ファイル:Koizumi-koshindo Konrinin Yamatokoriyama Nara pref Japan01n.jpg|300px|金輪院]] |所在地 = {{JPN}} 奈良県大和郡山市小泉町834 |位置 = |山号 = 尭然山 |宗旨 = |宗派 = 天台宗 |寺格 = |本尊 = 青面金剛 |創建年 = 万治2年（1659年） |開山 = |開基 = [[藤林宗源|藤林直良（宗源）]] |中興年 = |中興 = |正式名 = |別称 = 小泉庚申堂 |札所等 = |文化財 = 庭園、庫裏、表門、木造釈迦如来坐像（市指定） |公式HP = |公式HP名 = }} [[ファイル:Koizumi-koshindo Konrinin Yamatokoriyama Nara pref Japan02n.jpg|thumb|本堂]] '''金輪院'''（こんりんいん）は、[[奈良県]][[大和郡山市]]小泉町にある[[天台宗]]の寺院。
'''小泉庚申堂'''と呼称される方が多い。
山号は尭然山（ぎょうねんざん）、本尊は[[青面金剛]]。
[[大和国]]の[[庚申信仰]]の総道場として知られる。
== 歴史 == 万治2年（[[1659年]]）、[[小泉藩]]第2代藩主[[片桐貞昌]]の家臣で茶人でもあった[[藤林宗源|藤林直良（宗源）]]が創建し、小泉氏の祈願寺となった。
[[大徳寺]]玉舟和尚の筆による縁起「宗鐇記」によると、貞昌の父[[片桐貞隆]]が家蔵の什器を焼き捨てたとき、灰の中から青面金剛像一体が発見され、この像が後に本尊になったという。
明治5年8月施行の[[学制]]に基づき、同6年12月23日に山本寿保邸を借用して開校されていた小学修道館が、同7年2月当院倉庫へ教場が臨時移転し、同9年2月城行換邸に移転するまでの間使われた。
== 文化財 == [[ファイル:Koizumi-koshindo Konrinin Yamatokoriyama Nara pref Japan09n.jpg|thumb|扁額]] *[[扁額]] - 明治13年（[[1880年]]） 安村清一郎・森内彌三郎奉納。本堂に掲額。
== 交通アクセス == *[[大和路線]] [[大和小泉駅]]下車徒歩10分
== 周辺 == *[[慈光院]] *[[松尾寺 (大和郡山市)|松尾寺]]
== 関連項目 == {{Commonscat|Konrin-in}} *[[庚申堂]]
== 外部リンク == *[http://www.city.yamatokoriyama.nara.jp/report/machikado/002232.html 青面金剛とくくり猿の庚申堂に行ってきました。] - 大和郡山市まちかどレポート134 *[http://www.city.yamatokoriyama.nara.jp/kouhou/tektek/002810.html 大和小泉の庚申さん～市長てくてく城下町] - 大和郡山市広報つながり 平成24年6月1日号 No.1026

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[金輪院]] to [[Konrin-in]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
{{日本の寺院 |名称 = 金輪院 |画像 = [[ファイル:Koizumi-koshindo Konrinin Yamatokoriyama Nara pref Japan01n.jpg|300px|金輪院]] |所在地 = {{JPN}} 奈良県大和郡山市小泉町834 |位置 = |山号 = 尭然山 |宗旨 = |宗派 = 天台宗 |寺格 = |本尊 = 青面金剛 |創建年 = 万治2年（1659年） |開山 = |開基 = [[藤林宗源|藤林直良（宗源）]] |中興年 = |中興 = |正式名 = |別称 = 小泉庚申堂 |札所等 = |文化財 = 庭園、庫裏、表門、木造釈迦如来坐像（市指定） |公式HP = |公式HP名 = }} [[ファイル:Koizumi-koshindo Konrinin Yamatokoriyama Nara pref Japan02n.jpg|thumb|本堂]] '''金輪院'''（こんりんいん）は、[[奈良県]][[大和郡山市]]小泉町にある[[天台宗]]の寺院。
'''小泉庚申堂'''と呼称される方が多い。
山号は尭然山（ぎょうねんざん）、本尊は[[青面金剛]]。
[[大和国]]の[[庚申信仰]]の総道場として知られる。
== 歴史 == 万治2年（[[1659年]]）、[[小泉藩]]第2代藩主[[片桐貞昌]]の家臣で茶人でもあった[[藤林宗源|藤林直良（宗源）]]が創建し、小泉氏の祈願寺となった。
[[大徳寺]]玉舟和尚の筆による縁起「宗鐇記」によると、貞昌の父[[片桐貞隆]]が家蔵の什器を焼き捨てたとき、灰の中から青面金剛像一体が発見され、この像が後に本尊になったという。
明治5年8月施行の[[学制]]に基づき、同6年12月23日に山本寿保邸を借用して開校されていた小学修道館が、同7年2月当院倉庫へ教場が臨時移転し、同9年2月城行換邸に移転するまでの間使われた。
== 文化財 == [[ファイル:Koizumi-koshindo Konrinin Yamatokoriyama Nara pref Japan09n.jpg|thumb|扁額]] *[[扁額]] - 明治13年（[[1880年]]） 安村清一郎・森内彌三郎奉納。本堂に掲額。
== 交通アクセス == *[[大和路線]] [[大和小泉駅]]下車徒歩10分
== 周辺 == *[[慈光院]] *[[松尾寺 (大和郡山市)|松尾寺]]
== 関連項目 == {{Commonscat|Konrin-in}} *[[庚申堂]]
== 外部リンク == *[http://www.city.yamatokoriyama.nara.jp/report/machikado/002232.html 青面金剛とくくり猿の庚申堂に行ってきました。] - 大和郡山市まちかどレポート134 *[http://www.city.yamatokoriyama.nara.jp/kouhou/tektek/002810.html 大和小泉の庚申さん～市長てくてく城下町] - 大和郡山市広報つながり 平成24年6月1日号 No.1026
{{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|37|40|N|135|45|33|E|region:JP|display=title}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:こんりんいん}} [[Category:奈良県の寺]] [[Category:大和郡山市の歴史]] [[Category:大和郡山市の建築物]] [[Category:天台宗の寺院]] [[Category:庚申信仰]] [[Category:算額]]

{{infobox religious building |name = 金輪院|native_name = 金輪院 |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image = [[File:Koizumi-koshindo Konrinin Yamatokoriyama Nara pref Japan01n.jpg|300px|金輪院]] |address = {{JPN}} 奈良県大和郡山市小泉町834 |位置 = |山号 = 尭然山 |宗旨 = |宗派 = 天台宗 |寺格 = |本尊 = 青面金剛 |創建年 = 万治2nd year （1659th year ） |開山 = |開基 = [[藤林宗源|藤林直良（宗源）]] |中興年 = |中興 = |正式名 = |別称 = 小泉Kōshin-dō |札所等 = |文化財 = 庭園、庫裏、表門、木造釈迦如来坐像（市指定） |公式HP = |公式HP名 = }} [[File:Koizumi-koshindo Konrinin Yamatokoriyama Nara pref Japan02n.jpg|thumb|本堂]] '''Konrin-in''' (金輪院) is a temple of {{ill|Tendai Buddhism|ja|天台宗|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Yamatokoriyama|ja|大和郡山市|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Koizumi Town|ja|小泉町|qq=draft}}.
It is more commonly referred to as '''Koizumi Kōshin-dō'''.
Its mountain name is Gyōnen-zan (尭然山), and its principal image is {{ill|Aomen Kongo|ja|青面金剛|qq=draft}}.
It is known as the headquarters of the {{ill|Kōshin faith|ja|庚申信仰|qq=draft}} of {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province|qq=draft}}.
== History == In Manji 2 ({{ill|1659|ja|1659th year|qq=draft}}), a retainer and tea master of the 2nd lord of {{ill|Koizumi Domain|ja|小泉藩|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Sadamasa Katagiri|ja|片桐貞昌|qq=draft}}, established the temple, making it the wish-fulfilling temple (祈願寺) of the Koizumi family.
According to the origin legend "Sōryō-ki" written by the monk Gyokusen of {{ill|Daitoku-ji|ja|大徳寺|qq=draft}}, when Sadamasa's father, {{ill|Tadataka Katagiri|ja|片桐貞隆|qq=draft}}, burned and discarded the family's ritual implements, a single statue of {{ill|Aomen Kongo|ja|青面金剛|qq=draft}} was discovered amid the ashes; this statue later became the principal image.
Based on the Educational System enacted in Meiji 5, the elementary school Shūdōkan—which had been opened by borrowing the Yamamoto Jumpo Residence on December 23 of Meiji 6—was temporarily relocated to the temple's warehouse in February of Meiji 7, and remained in use until it was moved to the Jōkō Henge residence in February of Meiji 9.
== Cultural Properties == [[File:Koizumi-koshindo Konrinin Yamatokoriyama Nara pref Japan09n.jpg|thumb|Henga]] * {{ill|Henga|ja|扁額|qq=draft}} – Donated by Kiyoshiro Yasumura and Yasaburō Morinouchi in Meiji 13 (1880); hung in the main hall.
== Access == * On the {{ill|Yamato Line|ja|大和路線|qq=draft}}, it is a 10‑minute walk from {{ill|Yamato Koizumi Station|ja|大和小泉駅|qq=draft}}.
== Surroundings == * {{ill|Jikōin|ja|慈光院|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Matsuo-dera|ja|松尾寺 (大和郡山市)|lt=Matsuo Temple|qq=draft}}
== Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Konrin-in}} * {{ill|Kōshin-dō|ja|Kōshin-dō|qq=draft}}
== External Links == * [http://www.city.yamatokoriyama.nara.jp/report/machikado/002232.html I visited the Kōshin-dō of Aomen Kongo and Kukuri Saru] – Yamatokoriyama City Machikado Report 134 * [http://www.city.yamatokoriyama.nara.jp/kouhou/tektek/002810.html The Kōshin of Yamato Koizumi – A Mayor’s Walking Tour of the Castle Town] – Yamatokoriyama City Public Relations Tsunagari, Issue No.1026, June 1, Heisei 24
{{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|37|40|N|135|45|33|E|region:JP|display=title}} {{Buddhism-stub}}
{{translated page|ja|金輪院|version=95816798|comment=Imported full ja history}}

revision.comment: Moving from [[Category:天台宗の寺院]] to [[Category:Tiantai_temples]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{infobox religious building |name = 金輪院|native_name = 金輪院 |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image = [[File:Koizumi-koshindo Konrinin Yamatokoriyama Nara pref Japan01n.jpg|300px|金輪院]] |address = {{JPN}} 奈良県大和郡山市小泉町834 |位置 = |山号 = 尭然山 |宗旨 = |宗派 = 天台宗 |寺格 = |本尊 = 青面金剛 |創建年 = 万治2nd year （1659th year ） |開山 = |開基 = [[藤林宗源|藤林直良（宗源）]] |中興年 = |中興 = |正式名 = |別称 = 小泉Kōshin-dō |札所等 = |文化財 = 庭園、庫裏、表門、木造釈迦如来坐像（市指定） |公式HP = |公式HP名 = }} [[File:Koizumi-koshindo Konrinin Yamatokoriyama Nara pref Japan02n.jpg|thumb|本堂]] '''Konrin-in''' (金輪院) is a temple of {{ill|Tendai Buddhism|ja|天台宗|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Yamatokoriyama|ja|大和郡山市|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Koizumi Town|ja|小泉町|qq=draft}}.
It is more commonly referred to as '''Koizumi Kōshin-dō'''.
Its mountain name is Gyōnen-zan (尭然山), and its principal image is {{ill|Aomen Kongo|ja|青面金剛|qq=draft}}.
It is known as the headquarters of the {{ill|Kōshin faith|ja|庚申信仰|qq=draft}} of {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province|qq=draft}}.
== History == In Manji 2 ({{ill|1659|ja|1659th year|qq=draft}}), a retainer and tea master of the 2nd lord of {{ill|Koizumi Domain|ja|小泉藩|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Sadamasa Katagiri|ja|片桐貞昌|qq=draft}}, established the temple, making it the wish-fulfilling temple (祈願寺) of the Koizumi family.
According to the origin legend "Sōryō-ki" written by the monk Gyokusen of {{ill|Daitoku-ji|ja|大徳寺|qq=draft}}, when Sadamasa's father, {{ill|Tadataka Katagiri|ja|片桐貞隆|qq=draft}}, burned and discarded the family's ritual implements, a single statue of {{ill|Aomen Kongo|ja|青面金剛|qq=draft}} was discovered amid the ashes; this statue later became the principal image.
Based on the Educational System enacted in Meiji 5, the elementary school Shūdōkan—which had been opened by borrowing the Yamamoto Jumpo Residence on December 23 of Meiji 6—was temporarily relocated to the temple's warehouse in February of Meiji 7, and remained in use until it was moved to the Jōkō Henge residence in February of Meiji 9.
== Cultural Properties == [[File:Koizumi-koshindo Konrinin Yamatokoriyama Nara pref Japan09n.jpg|thumb|Henga]] * {{ill|Henga|ja|扁額|qq=draft}} – Donated by Kiyoshiro Yasumura and Yasaburō Morinouchi in Meiji 13 (1880); hung in the main hall.
== Access == * On the {{ill|Yamato Line|ja|大和路線|qq=draft}}, it is a 10‑minute walk from {{ill|Yamato Koizumi Station|ja|大和小泉駅|qq=draft}}.
== Surroundings == * {{ill|Jikōin|ja|慈光院|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Matsuo-dera|ja|松尾寺 (大和郡山市)|lt=Matsuo Temple|qq=draft}}
== Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Konrin-in}} * {{ill|Kōshin-dō|ja|Kōshin-dō|qq=draft}}
== External Links == * [http://www.city.yamatokoriyama.nara.jp/report/machikado/002232.html I visited the Kōshin-dō of Aomen Kongo and Kukuri Saru] – Yamatokoriyama City Machikado Report 134 * [http://www.city.yamatokoriyama.nara.jp/kouhou/tektek/002810.html The Kōshin of Yamato Koizumi – A Mayor’s Walking Tour of the Castle Town] – Yamatokoriyama City Public Relations Tsunagari, Issue No.1026, June 1, Heisei 24
{{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|37|40|N|135|45|33|E|region:JP|display=title}} {{Buddhism-stub}}
{{translated page|ja|金輪院|version=95816798|comment=Imported full ja history}}

[[Category:Completion tier 5]] }}

== Access == * On the {{ill|Yamato Line|ja|大和路線|qq=draft}}, it is a 10‑minute walk from {{ill|Yamato Koizumi Station|ja|大和小泉駅|qq=draft}}.
== Surroundings == * {{ill|Jikōin|ja|慈光院|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Matsuo-dera|ja|松尾寺 (大和郡山市)|lt=Matsuo Temple|qq=draft}}
== Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Konrin-in}} * {{ill|Kōshin-dō|ja|Kōshin-dō|qq=draft}}
== External Links == * [http://www.city.yamatokoriyama.nara.jp/report/machikado/002232.html I visited the Kōshin-dō of Aomen Kongo and Kukuri Saru] – Yamatokoriyama City Machikado Report 134 * [http://www.city.yamatokoriyama.nara.jp/kouhou/tektek/002810.html The Kōshin of Yamato Koizumi – A Mayor’s Walking Tour of the Castle Town] – Yamatokoriyama City Public Relations Tsunagari, Issue No.1026, June 1, Heisei 24

revision.text:
{{infobox religious building |name = 金輪院|native_name = 金輪院 |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image = [[File:Koizumi-koshindo Konrinin Yamatokoriyama Nara pref Japan01n.jpg|300px|金輪院]] |address = {{JPN}} 奈良県大和郡山市小泉町834 |位置 = |山号 = 尭然山 |宗旨 = |宗派 = 天台宗 |寺格 = |本尊 = 青面金剛 |創建年 = 万治2nd year （1659th year ） |開山 = |開基 = [[藤林宗源|藤林直良（宗源）]] |中興年 = |中興 = |正式名 = |別称 = 小泉Kōshin-dō |札所等 = |文化財 = 庭園、庫裏、表門、木造釈迦如来坐像（市指定） |公式HP = |公式HP名 = }} [[File:Koizumi-koshindo Konrinin Yamatokoriyama Nara pref Japan02n.jpg|thumb|本堂]] '''Konrin-in''' (金輪院) is a temple of {{ill|Tendai Buddhism|ja|天台宗|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tendai Buddhism|qq=}} located in {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefecture|qq=}}, {{ill|Yamatokoriyama|ja|大和郡山市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatokoriyama|qq=}}, {{ill|Koizumi Town|ja|小泉町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koizumi Town|qq=}}.
It is more commonly referred to as '''Koizumi Kōshin-dō'''.
Its mountain name is Gyōnen-zan (尭然山), and its principal image is {{ill|Aomen Kongo|ja|青面金剛|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aomen Kongo|qq=}}.
It is known as the headquarters of the {{ill|Kōshin faith|ja|庚申信仰|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin faith|qq=}} of {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Province|qq=}}.
== History == In Manji 2 ({{ill|1659|ja|1659th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1659|qq=}}), a retainer and tea master of the 2nd lord of {{ill|Koizumi Domain|ja|小泉藩|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koizumi Domain|qq=}}, {{ill|Sadamasa Katagiri|ja|片桐貞昌|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sadamasa Katagiri|qq=}}, established the temple, making it the wish-fulfilling temple (祈願寺) of the Koizumi family.
According to the origin legend "Sōryō-ki" written by the monk Gyokusen of {{ill|Daitoku-ji|ja|大徳寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daitoku-ji|qq=}}, when Sadamasa's father, {{ill|Tadataka Katagiri|ja|片桐貞隆|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tadataka Katagiri|qq=}}, burned and discarded the family's ritual implements, a single statue of {{ill|Aomen Kongo|ja|青面金剛|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aomen Kongo|qq=}} was discovered amid the ashes; this statue later became the principal image.
Based on the Educational System enacted in Meiji 5, the elementary school Shūdōkan—which had been opened by borrowing the Yamamoto Jumpo Residence on December 23 of Meiji 6—was temporarily relocated to the temple's warehouse in February of Meiji 7, and remained in use until it was moved to the Jōkō Henge residence in February of Meiji 9.
== Cultural Properties == [[File:Koizumi-koshindo Konrinin Yamatokoriyama Nara pref Japan09n.jpg|thumb|Henga]] * {{ill|Henga|ja|扁額|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Henga|qq=}} – Donated by Kiyoshiro Yasumura and Yasaburō Morinouchi in Meiji 13 (1880); hung in the main hall.
== Access == * On the {{ill|Yamato Line|ja|大和路線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Line|qq=}}, it is a 10‑minute walk from {{ill|Yamato Koizumi Station|ja|大和小泉駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Koizumi Station|qq=}}.
== Surroundings == * {{ill|Jikōin|ja|慈光院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jikōin|qq=}} * {{ill|Matsuo-dera|ja|松尾寺 (大和郡山市)|lt=Matsuo Temple|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuo-dera|qq=}}
== Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Konrin-in}} * {{ill|Kōshin-dō|ja|Kōshin-dō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin-dō|qq=}}
== External Links == * [http://www.city.yamatokoriyama.nara.jp/report/machikado/002232.html I visited the Kōshin-dō of Aomen Kongo and Kukuri Saru] – Yamatokoriyama City Machikado Report 134 * [http://www.city.yamatokoriyama.nara.jp/kouhou/tektek/002810.html The Kōshin of Yamato Koizumi – A Mayor’s Walking Tour of the Castle Town] – Yamatokoriyama City Public Relations Tsunagari, Issue No.1026, June 1, Heisei 24
{{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|37|40|N|135|45|33|E|region:JP|display=title}} {{Buddhism-stub}}
{{translated page|ja|金輪院|version=95816798|comment=Imported full ja history}}

{{infobox religious building |name = 金輪院|native_name = 金輪院 |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image = [[File:Koizumi-koshindo Konrinin Yamatokoriyama Nara pref Japan01n.jpg|300px|金輪院]] |address = {{JPN}} 奈良県大和郡山市小泉町834 |位置 = |山号 = 尭然山 |宗旨 = |宗派 = 天台宗 |寺格 = |本尊 = 青面金剛 |創建年 = 万治2nd year （1659th year ） |開山 = |開基 = [[藤林宗源|藤林直良（宗源）]] |中興年 = |中興 = |正式名 = |別称 = 小泉Kōshin-dō |札所等 = |文化財 = 庭園、庫裏、表門、木造釈迦如来坐像（市指定） |公式HP = |公式HP名 = }} [[File:Koizumi-koshindo Konrinin Yamatokoriyama Nara pref Japan02n.jpg|thumb|本堂]] '''Konrin-in''' (金輪院) is a temple of {{ill|Tendai Buddhism|ja|天台宗|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tendai Buddhism|qq=}} located in {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefecture|qq=}}, {{ill|Yamatokoriyama|ja|大和郡山市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatokoriyama|qq=}}, {{ill|Koizumi Town|ja|小泉町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koizumi Town|qq=}}.
It is more commonly referred to as '''Koizumi Kōshin-dō'''.
Its mountain name is Gyōnen-zan (尭然山), and its principal image is {{ill|Aomen Kongo|ja|青面金剛|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aomen Kongo|qq=}}.
It is known as the headquarters of the {{ill|Kōshin|lt=Kōshin|lt=Kōshin faith|ja|庚申信仰|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin faith|qq=}} of {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国|qq=Yamato Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Province|qq=}}.
== History == In Manji 2 ({{ill|1659|ja|1659th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1659|qq=}}), a retainer and tea master of the 2nd lord of {{ill|Koizumi Domain|ja|小泉藩|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koizumi Domain|qq=}}, {{ill|Sadamasa Katagiri|ja|片桐貞昌|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sadamasa Katagiri|qq=}}, established the temple, making it the wish-fulfilling temple (祈願寺) of the Koizumi family.
According to the origin legend "Sōryō-ki" written by the monk Gyokusen of {{ill|Daitoku-ji|ja|大徳寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daitoku-ji|qq=}}, when Sadamasa's father, {{ill|Tadataka Katagiri|ja|片桐貞隆|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tadataka Katagiri|qq=}}, burned and discarded the family's ritual implements, a single statue of {{ill|Aomen Kongo|ja|青面金剛|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aomen Kongo|qq=}} was discovered amid the ashes; this statue later became the principal image.
Based on the Educational System enacted in Meiji 5, the elementary school Shūdōkan—which had been opened by borrowing the Yamamoto Jumpo Residence on December 23 of Meiji 6—was temporarily relocated to the temple's warehouse in February of Meiji 7, and remained in use until it was moved to the Jōkō Henge residence in February of Meiji 9.
== Cultural Properties == [[File:Koizumi-koshindo Konrinin Yamatokoriyama Nara pref Japan09n.jpg|thumb|Henga]] * {{ill|Henga|ja|扁額|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Henga|qq=}} – Donated by Kiyoshiro Yasumura and Yasaburō Morinouchi in Meiji 13 (1880); hung in the main hall.
== Access == * On the {{ill|Yamato Line|ja|大和路線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Line|qq=}}, it is a 10‑minute walk from {{ill|Yamato Koizumi Station|ja|大和小泉駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Koizumi Station|qq=}}.
== Surroundings == * {{ill|Jikōin|ja|慈光院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jikōin|qq=}} * {{ill|Matsuo-dera|ja|松尾寺 (大和郡山市)|lt=Matsuo Temple|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuo-dera|qq=}}
== Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Konrin-in}} * {{ill|Kōshin-dō|ja|Kōshin-dō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin-dō|qq=}}
== External Links == * [http://www.city.yamatokoriyama.nara.jp/report/machikado/002232.html I visited the Kōshin-dō of Aomen Kongo and Kukuri Saru] – Yamatokoriyama City Machikado Report 134 * [http://www.city.yamatokoriyama.nara.jp/kouhou/tektek/002810.html The Kōshin of Yamato Koizumi – A Mayor’s Walking Tour of the Castle Town] – Yamatokoriyama City Public Relations Tsunagari, Issue No.1026, June 1, Heisei 24

== Cultural Properties == [[File:Koizumi-koshindo Konrinin Yamatokoriyama Nara pref Japan09n.jpg|thumb|Henga]] * {{ill|Henga|ja|扁額|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Henga|qq=}} – Donated by Kiyoshiro Yasumura and Yasaburō Morinouchi in Meiji 13 (1880); hung in the main hall.
== Access == * On the [[:en:Yamatoji Line|Yamato Line]], it is a 10‑minute walk from [[:en:Yamato-Koizumi Station|Yamato Koizumi Station]].
== Surroundings == * [[:en:Jikō-in|Jikōin]] * {{ill|Matsuo-dera|ja|松尾寺 (大和郡山市)|lt=Matsuo Temple|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuo-dera|qq=}}

== History == In Manji 2 ([[:en:1659|1659]]), a retainer and tea master of the 2nd lord of [[:en:Koizumi Domain|Koizumi Domain]], {{ill|Sadamasa Katagiri|ja|片桐貞昌|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sadamasa Katagiri|qq=}}, established the temple, making it the wish-fulfilling temple (祈願寺) of the Koizumi family.
According to the origin legend "Sōryō-ki" written by the monk Gyokusen of [[:en:Daitoku-ji|Daitoku-ji]], when Sadamasa's father, {{ill|Tadataka Katagiri|ja|片桐貞隆|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tadataka Katagiri|qq=}}, burned and discarded the family's ritual implements, a single statue of {{ill|Aomen Kongo|ja|青面金剛|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aomen Kongo|qq=}} was discovered amid the ashes; this statue later became the principal image.
Based on the Educational System enacted in Meiji 5, the elementary school Shūdōkan—which had been opened by borrowing the Yamamoto Jumpo Residence on December 23 of Meiji 6—was temporarily relocated to the temple's warehouse in February of Meiji 7, and remained in use until it was moved to the Jōkō Henge residence in February of Meiji 9.
== Cultural Properties == [[File:Koizumi-koshindo Konrinin Yamatokoriyama Nara pref Japan09n.jpg|thumb|Henga]] * {{ill|Henga|ja|扁額|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Henga|qq=}} – Donated by Kiyoshiro Yasumura and Yasaburō Morinouchi in Meiji 13 (1880); hung in the main hall.
== Access == * On the [[:en:Yamatoji Line|Yamato Line]], it is a 10‑minute walk from [[:en:Yamato-Koizumi Station|Yamato Koizumi Station]].
== Surroundings == * [[:en:Jikō-in|Jikōin]] * {{ill|Matsuo-dera|ja|松尾寺 (大和郡山市)|lt=Matsuo Temple|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuo-dera|qq=}}
== Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Konrin-in}} * {{ill|Kōshin-dō|ja|Kōshin-dō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin-dō|qq=}}
== External Links == * [http://www.city.yamatokoriyama.nara.jp/report/machikado/002232.html I visited the Kōshin-dō of Aomen Kongo and Kukuri Saru] – Yamatokoriyama City Machikado Report 134 * [http://www.city.yamatokoriyama.nara.jp/kouhou/tektek/002810.html The Kōshin of Yamato Koizumi – A Mayor’s Walking Tour of the Castle Town] – Yamatokoriyama City Public Relations Tsunagari, Issue No.1026, June 1, Heisei 24

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Konrin-in]][[ja:金輪院]] {{Infobox Buddhist temple|image=File:Koizumi-koshindo Konrinin Yamatokoriyama Nara pref Japan01n.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Yamatokoriyama|WD=Q118213}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.627778|N|135.759167|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11647941]] Wikidata name: Konrin-in [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11647941]] Wikidata name: Konrin-in [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox religious building |name = 金輪院|native_name = 金輪院 |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image = [[File:Koizumi-koshindo Konrinin Yamatokoriyama Nara pref Japan01n.jpg|300px|金輪院]] |address = {{JPN}} 奈良県大和郡山市小泉町834 |位置 = |山号 = 尭然山 |宗旨 = |宗派 = 天台宗 |寺格 = |本尊 = 青面金剛 |創建年 = 万治2nd year （1659th year ） |開山 = |開基 = [[藤林宗源|藤林直良（宗源）]] |中興年 = |中興 = |正式名 = |別称 = 小泉Kōshin-dō |札所等 = |文化財 = 庭園、庫裏、表門、木造釈迦如来坐像（市指定） |公式HP = |公式HP名 = }} [[File:Koizumi-koshindo Konrinin Yamatokoriyama Nara pref Japan02n.jpg|thumb|本堂]] '''Konrin-in''' (金輪院) is a temple of [[:en:Tiantai|Tendai Buddhism]] located in [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Yamatokōriyama|Yamatokoriyama]], {{ill|Koizumi Town|ja|小泉町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koizumi Town|qq=|1=Koizumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11462551}}.
It is more commonly referred to as '''Koizumi Kōshin-dō'''.
Its mountain name is Gyōnen-zan (尭然山), and its principal image is {{ill|Aomen Kongo|ja|青面金剛|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aomen Kongo|qq=|1=Shōmen Kongō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11662826}}.
It is known as the headquarters of the [[:en:Kōshin|Kōshin faith]] of [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]].
== History == In Manji 2 ([[:en:1659|1659]]), a retainer and tea master of the 2nd lord of [[:en:Koizumi Domain|Koizumi Domain]], {{ill|Sadamasa Katagiri|ja|片桐貞昌|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sadamasa Katagiri|qq=|1=Katagiri Sadamasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11569883}}, established the temple, making it the wish-fulfilling temple (祈願寺) of the Koizumi family.
According to the origin legend "Sōryō-ki" written by the monk Gyokusen of [[:en:Daitoku-ji|Daitoku-ji]], when Sadamasa's father, {{ill|Tadataka Katagiri|ja|片桐貞隆|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tadataka Katagiri|qq=|1=Tadataka Katagiri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11569889}}, burned and discarded the family's ritual implements, a single statue of {{ill|Aomen Kongo|ja|青面金剛|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aomen Kongo|qq=|1=Shōmen Kongō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11662826}} was discovered amid the ashes; this statue later became the principal image.
Based on the Educational System enacted in Meiji 5, the elementary school Shūdōkan—which had been opened by borrowing the Yamamoto Jumpo Residence on December 23 of Meiji 6—was temporarily relocated to the temple's warehouse in February of Meiji 7, and remained in use until it was moved to the Jōkō Henge residence in February of Meiji 9.
== Cultural Properties == [[File:Koizumi-koshindo Konrinin Yamatokoriyama Nara pref Japan09n.jpg|thumb|Henga]] * {{ill|Henga|ja|扁額|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Henga|qq=|1=biane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5358873}} – Donated by Kiyoshiro Yasumura and Yasaburō Morinouchi in Meiji 13 (1880); hung in the main hall.
== Access == * On the [[:en:Yamatoji Line|Yamato Line]], it is a 10‑minute walk from [[:en:Yamato-Koizumi Station|Yamato Koizumi Station]].
== Surroundings == * [[:en:Jikō-in|Jikōin]] * {{ill|Matsuo-dera|ja|松尾寺 (大和郡山市)|lt=Matsuo Temple|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuo-dera|qq=|1=Matsuo-dera|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11529606}}
== Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Konrin-in}} * {{ill|Kōshin-dō|ja|Kōshin-dō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin-dō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Konrin-in]][[ja:金輪院]] {{Infobox Buddhist temple|image=File:Koizumi-koshindo Konrinin Yamatokoriyama Nara pref Japan01n.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Yamatokoriyama|WD=Q118213}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.627778|N|135.759167|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11647941]] Wikidata name: Konrin-in [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11647941]] Wikidata name: Konrin-in [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox religious building |name = 金輪院|native_name = 金輪院 |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image = [[File:Koizumi-koshindo Konrinin Yamatokoriyama Nara pref Japan01n.jpg|300px|金輪院]] |address = {{JPN}} 奈良県大和郡山市小泉町834 |位置 = |山号 = 尭然山 |宗旨 = |宗派 = 天台宗 |寺格 = |本尊 = 青面金剛 |創建年 = 万治2nd year （1659th year ） |開山 = |開基 = [[藤林宗源|藤林直良（宗源）]] |中興年 = |中興 = |正式名 = |別称 = 小泉Kōshin-dō |札所等 = |文化財 = 庭園、庫裏、表門、木造釈迦如来坐像（市指定） |公式HP = |公式HP名 = }} [[File:Koizumi-koshindo Konrinin Yamatokoriyama Nara pref Japan02n.jpg|thumb|本堂]] '''Konrin-in''' (金輪院) is a temple of [[:en:Tiantai|Tendai Buddhism]] located in [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Yamatokōriyama|Yamatokoriyama]], {{ill|Koizumi Town|ja|小泉町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koizumi Town|1=Koizumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11462551}}.
It is more commonly referred to as '''Koizumi Kōshin-dō'''.
Its mountain name is Gyōnen-zan (尭然山), and its principal image is {{ill|Aomen Kongo|ja|青面金剛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aomen Kongo|1=Shōmen Kongō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11662826}}.
It is known as the headquarters of the [[:en:Kōshin|Kōshin faith]] of [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]].
== History == In Manji 2 ([[:en:1659|1659]]), a retainer and tea master of the 2nd lord of [[:en:Koizumi Domain|Koizumi Domain]], {{ill|Sadamasa Katagiri|ja|片桐貞昌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sadamasa Katagiri|1=Katagiri Sadamasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11569883}}, established the temple, making it the wish-fulfilling temple (祈願寺) of the Koizumi family.
According to the origin legend "Sōryō-ki" written by the monk Gyokusen of [[:en:Daitoku-ji|Daitoku-ji]], when Sadamasa's father, {{ill|Tadataka Katagiri|ja|片桐貞隆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tadataka Katagiri|1=Tadataka Katagiri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11569889}}, burned and discarded the family's ritual implements, a single statue of {{ill|Aomen Kongo|ja|青面金剛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aomen Kongo|1=Shōmen Kongō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11662826}} was discovered amid the ashes; this statue later became the principal image.
Based on the Educational System enacted in Meiji 5, the elementary school Shūdōkan—which had been opened by borrowing the Yamamoto Jumpo Residence on December 23 of Meiji 6—was temporarily relocated to the temple's warehouse in February of Meiji 7, and remained in use until it was moved to the Jōkō Henge residence in February of Meiji 9.
== Cultural Properties == [[File:Koizumi-koshindo Konrinin Yamatokoriyama Nara pref Japan09n.jpg|thumb|Henga]] * {{ill|Henga|ja|扁額|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Henga|1=biane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5358873}} – Donated by Kiyoshiro Yasumura and Yasaburō Morinouchi in Meiji 13 (1880); hung in the main hall.
== Access == * On the [[:en:Yamatoji Line|Yamato Line]], it is a 10‑minute walk from [[:en:Yamato-Koizumi Station|Yamato Koizumi Station]].
== Surroundings == * [[:en:Jikō-in|Jikōin]] * {{ill|Matsuo-dera|ja|松尾寺 (大和郡山市)|lt=Matsuo Temple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuo-dera|1=Matsuo-dera|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11529606}}
== Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Konrin-in}} * {{ill|Kōshin-dō|ja|Kōshin-dō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin-dō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
== External Links == * [http://www.city.yamatokoriyama.nara.jp/report/machikado/002232.html I visited the Kōshin-dō of Aomen Kongo and Kukuri Saru] – Yamatokoriyama City Machikado Report 134 * [http://www.city.yamatokoriyama.nara.jp/kouhou/tektek/002810.html The Kōshin of Yamato Koizumi – A Mayor’s Walking Tour of the Castle Town] – Yamatokoriyama City Public Relations Tsunagari, Issue No.1026, June 1, Heisei 24

Based on the Educational System enacted in Meiji 5, the elementary school Shūdōkan—which had been opened by borrowing the Yamamoto Jumpo Residence on December 23 of Meiji 6—was temporarily relocated to the temple's warehouse in February of Meiji 7, and remained in use until it was moved to the Jōkō Henge residence in February of Meiji 9.

== Access == * On the [[:en:Yamatoji Line|Yamato Line]], it is a 10‑minute walk from [[:en:Yamato-Koizumi Station|Yamato Koizumi Station]].
== Surroundings == * [[:en:Jikō-in|Jikōin]] * {{ill|Matsuo-dera|ja|松尾寺 (大和郡山市)|lt=Matsuo Temple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuo-dera|1=Matsuo-dera|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11529606}}
== Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Konrin-in}} * {{ill|Kōshin-dō|ja|Kōshin-dō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin-dō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Konrin-in]][[ja:金輪院]] {{Infobox Buddhist temple|image=File:Koizumi-koshindo Konrinin Yamatokoriyama Nara pref Japan01n.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Yamatokoriyama|WD=Q118213}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.627778|N|135.759167|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11647941]] Wikidata name: Konrin-in [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11647941]] Wikidata name: Konrin-in [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox religious building |name = 金輪院|native_name = 金輪院 |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image = [[File:Koizumi-koshindo Konrinin Yamatokoriyama Nara pref Japan01n.jpg|300px|金輪院]] |address = {{JPN}} 奈良県大和郡山市小泉町834 |位置 = |山号 = 尭然山 |宗旨 = |宗派 = 天台宗 |寺格 = |本尊 = 青面金剛 |創建年 = 万治2nd year （1659th year ） |開山 = |開基 = [[藤林宗源|藤林直良（宗源）]] |中興年 = |中興 = |正式名 = |別称 = 小泉Kōshin-dō |札所等 = |文化財 = 庭園、庫裏、表門、木造釈迦如来坐像（市指定） |公式HP = |公式HP名 = }} [[File:Koizumi-koshindo Konrinin Yamatokoriyama Nara pref Japan02n.jpg|thumb|本堂]] '''Konrin-in''' (金輪院) is a temple of [[:en:Tiantai|Tendai Buddhism]] located in [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Yamatokōriyama|Yamatokoriyama]], {{ill|Koizumi Town|ja|小泉町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koizumi Town|1=Koizumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11462551}}.
It is more commonly referred to as '''Koizumi Kōshin-dō'''.
Its mountain name is Gyōnen-zan (尭然山), and its principal image is {{ill|Aomen Kongo|ja|青面金剛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aomen Kongo|1=Shōmen Kongō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11662826}}.
It is known as the headquarters of the [[:en:Kōshin|Kōshin faith]] of [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]].
== History == In Manji 2 ([[:en:1659|1659]]), a retainer and tea master of the 2nd lord of [[:en:Koizumi Domain|Koizumi Domain]], {{ill|Sadamasa Katagiri|ja|片桐貞昌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sadamasa Katagiri|1=Katagiri Sadamasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11569883}}, established the temple, making it the wish-fulfilling temple (祈願寺) of the Koizumi family.
According to the origin legend "Sōryō-ki" written by the monk Gyokusen of [[:en:Daitoku-ji|Daitoku-ji]], when Sadamasa's father, {{ill|Tadataka Katagiri|ja|片桐貞隆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tadataka Katagiri|1=Tadataka Katagiri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11569889}}, burned and discarded the family's ritual implements, a single statue of {{ill|Aomen Kongo|ja|青面金剛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aomen Kongo|1=Shōmen Kongō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11662826}} was discovered amid the ashes; this statue later became the principal image.
Based on the Educational System enacted in Meiji 5, the elementary school Shūdōkan—which had been opened by borrowing the Yamamoto Jumpo Residence on December 23 of Meiji 6—was temporarily relocated to the temple's warehouse in February of Meiji 7, and remained in use until it was moved to the Jōkō Henge residence in February of Meiji 9.
== Cultural Properties == [[File:Koizumi-koshindo Konrinin Yamatokoriyama Nara pref Japan09n.jpg|thumb|Henga]] * {{ill|Henga|ja|扁額|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Henga|1=biane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5358873}} – Donated by Kiyoshiro Yasumura and Yasaburō Morinouchi in Meiji 13 (1880); hung in the main hall.
== Access == * On the [[:en:Yamatoji Line|Yamato Line]], it is a 10‑minute walk from [[:en:Yamato-Koizumi Station|Yamato Koizumi Station]].
== Surroundings == * [[:en:Jikō-in|Jikōin]] * {{ill|Matsuo-dera|ja|松尾寺 (大和郡山市)|lt=Matsuo Temple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuo-dera|1=Matsuo-dera|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11529606}}
== Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Konrin-in}} * {{ill|Kōshin-dō|ja|Kōshin-dō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin-dō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

== History == In Manji 2 ([[:en:1659|1659]]), a retainer and tea master of the 2nd lord of [[:en:Koizumi Domain|Koizumi Domain]], {{ill|Sadamasa Katagiri|ja|片桐貞昌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sadamasa Katagiri|1=Katagiri Sadamasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11569883}}, established the temple, making it the wish-fulfilling temple (祈願寺) of the Koizumi family.
According to the origin legend "Sōryō-ki" written by the monk Gyokusen of [[:en:Daitoku-ji|Daitoku-ji]], when Sadamasa's father, {{ill|Tadataka Katagiri|ja|片桐貞隆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tadataka Katagiri|1=Tadataka Katagiri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11569889}}, burned and discarded the family's ritual implements, a single statue of {{ill|Aomen Kongo|ja|青面金剛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aomen Kongo|1=Shōmen Kongō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11662826}} was discovered amid the ashes; this statue later became the principal image.
Based on the Educational System enacted in Meiji 5, the elementary school Shūdōkan—which had been opened by borrowing the Yamamoto Jumpo Residence on December 23 of Meiji 6—was temporarily relocated to the temple's warehouse in February of Meiji 7, and remained in use until it was moved to the Jōkō Henge residence in February of Meiji 9.
== Cultural Properties == [[File:Koizumi-koshindo Konrinin Yamatokoriyama Nara pref Japan09n.jpg|thumb|Henga]] * {{ill|Henga|ja|扁額|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Henga|1=biane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5358873}} – Donated by Kiyoshiro Yasumura and Yasaburō Morinouchi in Meiji 13 (1880); hung in the main hall.
== Access == * On the [[:en:Yamatoji Line|Yamato Line]], it is a 10‑minute walk from [[:en:Yamato-Koizumi Station|Yamato Koizumi Station]].
== Surroundings == * [[:en:Jikō-in|Jikōin]] * {{ill|Matsuo-dera|ja|松尾寺 (大和郡山市)|lt=Matsuo Temple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuo-dera|1=Matsuo-dera|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11529606}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Konrin-in]][[ja:金輪院]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Konrin-in]][[ja:金輪院]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Konrin-in]][[ja:金輪院]] {{Infobox Buddhist temple|image=File:Koizumi-koshindo Konrinin Yamatokoriyama Nara pref Japan01n.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Yamatokoriyama|WD=Q118213}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.627778|N|135.759167|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11647941]] Wikidata name: Konrin-in [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11647941]] Wikidata name: Konrin-in [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox religious building |name = 金輪院|native_name = 金輪院 |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image = [[File:Koizumi-koshindo Konrinin Yamatokoriyama Nara pref Japan01n.jpg|300px|金輪院]] |address = {{JPN}} 奈良県大和郡山市小泉町834 |位置 = |山号 = 尭然山 |宗旨 = |宗派 = 天台宗 |寺格 = |本尊 = 青面金剛 |創建年 = 万治2nd year （1659th year ） |開山 = |開基 = [[藤林宗源|藤林直良（宗源）]] |中興年 = |中興 = |正式名 = |別称 = 小泉Kōshin-dō |札所等 = |文化財 = 庭園、庫裏、表門、木造釈迦如来坐像（市指定） |公式HP = |公式HP名 = }} [[File:Koizumi-koshindo Konrinin Yamatokoriyama Nara pref Japan02n.jpg|thumb|本堂]] '''Konrin-in''' (金輪院) is a temple of [[:en:Tiantai|Tendai Buddhism]] located in [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Yamatokōriyama|Yamatokoriyama]], {{ill|Koizumi Town|ja|小泉町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koizumi Town|1=Koizumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11462551}}.
It is more commonly referred to as '''Koizumi Kōshin-dō'''.
Its mountain name is Gyōnen-zan (尭然山), and its principal image is {{ill|Aomen Kongo|ja|青面金剛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aomen Kongo|1=Shōmen Kongō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11662826}}.
It is known as the headquarters of the [[:en:Kōshin|Kōshin faith]] of [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]].
== History == In Manji 2 ([[:en:1659|1659]]), a retainer and tea master of the 2nd lord of [[:en:Koizumi Domain|Koizumi Domain]], {{ill|Sadamasa Katagiri|ja|片桐貞昌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sadamasa Katagiri|1=Katagiri Sadamasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11569883}}, established the temple, making it the wish-fulfilling temple (祈願寺) of the Koizumi family.
According to the origin legend "Sōryō-ki" written by the monk Gyokusen of [[:en:Daitoku-ji|Daitoku-ji]], when Sadamasa's father, {{ill|Tadataka Katagiri|ja|片桐貞隆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tadataka Katagiri|1=Tadataka Katagiri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11569889}}, burned and discarded the family's ritual implements, a single statue of {{ill|Aomen Kongo|ja|青面金剛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aomen Kongo|1=Shōmen Kongō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11662826}} was discovered amid the ashes; this statue later became the principal image.
Based on the Educational System enacted in Meiji 5, the elementary school Shūdōkan—which had been opened by borrowing the Yamamoto Jumpo Residence on December 23 of Meiji 6—was temporarily relocated to the temple's warehouse in February of Meiji 7, and remained in use until it was moved to the Jōkō Henge residence in February of Meiji 9.
== Cultural Properties == [[File:Koizumi-koshindo Konrinin Yamatokoriyama Nara pref Japan09n.jpg|thumb|Henga]] * {{ill|Henga|ja|扁額|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Henga|1=biane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5358873}} – Donated by Kiyoshiro Yasumura and Yasaburō Morinouchi in Meiji 13 (1880); hung in the main hall.
== Access == * On the [[:en:Yamatoji Line|Yamato Line]], it is a 10‑minute walk from [[:en:Yamato-Koizumi Station|Yamato Koizumi Station]].
== Surroundings == * [[:en:Jikō-in|Jikōin]] * {{ill|Matsuo-dera|ja|松尾寺 (大和郡山市)|lt=Matsuo Temple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuo-dera|1=Matsuo-dera|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11529606}}
== Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Konrin-in}} * {{ill|Kōshin-dō|ja|Kōshin-dō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin-dō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
== External Links == * [http://www.city.yamatokoriyama.nara.jp/report/machikado/002232.html I visited the Kōshin-dō of Aomen Kongo and Kukuri Saru] – Yamatokoriyama City Machikado Report 134 * [http://www.city.yamatokoriyama.nara.jp/kouhou/tektek/002810.html The Kōshin of Yamato Koizumi – A Mayor’s Walking Tour of the Castle Town] – Yamatokoriyama City Public Relations Tsunagari, Issue No.1026, June 1, Heisei 24

It is known as the headquarters of the {{ill|Kōshin|en|Kōshin|WD=Q3082157|lt=Kōshin faith}} of {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province}}.
== History == In Manji 2 ({{ill|1659|en|1659|WD=Q6995|lt=1659}}), a retainer and tea master of the 2nd lord of {{ill|Koizumi Domain|en|Koizumi Domain|WD=Q1005257|lt=Koizumi Domain}}, {{ill|Sadamasa Katagiri|ja|片桐貞昌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sadamasa Katagiri|1=Katagiri Sadamasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11569883}}, established the temple, making it the wish-fulfilling temple (祈願寺) of the Koizumi family.

== History == In Manji 2 ({{ill|1659|en|1659|WD=Q6995|lt=1659}}), a retainer and tea master of the 2nd lord of {{ill|Koizumi Domain|en|Koizumi Domain|WD=Q1005257|lt=Koizumi Domain}}, {{ill|Sadamasa Katagiri|ja|片桐貞昌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sadamasa Katagiri|1=Katagiri Sadamasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11569883}}, established the temple, making it the wish-fulfilling temple (祈願寺) of the Koizumi family.
According to the origin legend "Sōryō-ki" written by the monk Gyokusen of {{ill|Daitoku-ji|en|Daitoku-ji|WD=Q901256|lt=Daitoku-ji}}, when Sadamasa's father, {{ill|Tadataka Katagiri|ja|片桐貞隆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tadataka Katagiri|1=Tadataka Katagiri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11569889}}, burned and discarded the family's ritual implements, a single statue of {{ill|Aomen Kongo|ja|青面金剛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aomen Kongo|1=Shōmen Kongō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11662826}} was discovered amid the ashes; this statue later became the principal image.
Based on the Educational System enacted in Meiji 5, the elementary school Shūdōkan—which had been opened by borrowing the Yamamoto Jumpo Residence on December 23 of Meiji 6—was temporarily relocated to the temple's warehouse in February of Meiji 7, and remained in use until it was moved to the Jōkō Henge residence in February of Meiji 9.
== Cultural Properties == [[File:Koizumi-koshindo Konrinin Yamatokoriyama Nara pref Japan09n.jpg|thumb|Henga]] * {{ill|Henga|ja|扁額|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Henga|1=biane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5358873}} – Donated by Kiyoshiro Yasumura and Yasaburō Morinouchi in Meiji 13 (1880); hung in the main hall.
== Access == * On the {{ill|Yamatoji Line|en|Yamatoji Line|WD=Q1151659|lt=Yamato Line}}, it is a 10‑minute walk from {{ill|Yamato-Koizumi Station|en|Yamato-Koizumi Station|WD=Q856532|lt=Yamato Koizumi Station}}.
== Surroundings == * {{ill|Jikō-in|en|Jikō-in|WD=Q11494987|lt=Jikōin}} * {{ill|Matsuo-dera|ja|松尾寺 (大和郡山市)|lt=Matsuo Temple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuo-dera|1=Matsuo-dera|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11529606}}
== Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Konrin-in}} * {{ill|Kōshin-dō|ja|Kōshin-dō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin-dō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

{{infobox religious building |name = 金輪院|native_name = 金輪院 |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image = [[File:Koizumi-koshindo Konrinin Yamatokoriyama Nara pref Japan01n.jpg|300px|金輪院]] |address = {{JPN}} 奈良県大和郡山市小泉町834 |位置 = |山号 = 尭然山 |宗旨 = |宗派 = 天台宗 |寺格 = |本尊 = 青面金剛 |創建年 = 万治2nd year （1659th year ） |開山 = |開基 = [[藤林宗源|藤林直良（宗源）]] |中興年 = |中興 = |正式名 = |別称 = 小泉Kōshin-dō |札所等 = |文化財 = 庭園、庫裏、表門、木造釈迦如来坐像（市指定） |公式HP = |公式HP名 = }} [[File:Koizumi-koshindo Konrinin Yamatokoriyama Nara pref Japan02n.jpg|thumb|本堂]] '''Konrin-in''' (金輪院) is a temple of {{ill|Tiantai|en|Tiantai|WD=Q668188|lt=Tendai Buddhism}} located in {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}, {{ill|Yamatokōriyama|en|Yamatokōriyama|WD=Q118213|lt=Yamatokoriyama}}, {{ill|Koizumi Town|ja|小泉町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koizumi Town|1=Koizumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11462551}}.

It is known as the headquarters of the {{ill|Kōshin|en|Kōshin|WD=Q3082157|lt=Kōshin faith}} of {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province}}.

Based on the Educational System enacted in Meiji 5, the elementary school Shūdōkan—which had been opened by borrowing the Yamamoto Jumpo Residence on December 23 of Meiji 6—was temporarily relocated to the temple's warehouse in February of Meiji 7, and remained in use until it was moved to the Jōkō Henge residence in February of Meiji 9.
