{{infobox religious building |name = 金輪院|native_name = 金輪院 |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image = [[File:Koizumi-koshindo Konrinin Yamatokoriyama Nara pref Japan01n.jpg|300px|金輪院]] |address = {{JPN}} 奈良県大和郡山市小泉町834 |位置 = |山号 = 尭然山 |宗旨 = |宗派 = 天台宗 |寺格 = |本尊 = 青面金剛 |創建年 = 万治2nd year （1659th year ） |開山 = |開基 = [[藤林宗源|藤林直良（宗源）]] |中興年 = |中興 = |正式名 = |別称 = 小泉Kōshin-dō |札所等 = |文化財 = 庭園、庫裏、表門、木造釈迦如来坐像（市指定） |公式HP = |公式HP名 = }} [[File:Koizumi-koshindo Konrinin Yamatokoriyama Nara pref Japan02n.jpg|thumb|本堂]] '''Konrin-in''' (金輪院) is a temple of {{ill|Tiantai|en|Tiantai|WD=Q668188|lt=Tendai Buddhism}} located in {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}, {{ill|Yamatokōriyama|en|Yamatokōriyama|WD=Q118213|lt=Yamatokoriyama}}, {{ill|Koizumi Town|ja|小泉町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koizumi Town|1=Koizumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11462551}}.
It is more commonly referred to as '''Koizumi Kōshin-dō'''.
Its mountain name is Gyōnen-zan (尭然山), and its principal image is {{ill|Aomen Kongo|ja|青面金剛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aomen Kongo|1=Shōmen Kongō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11662826}}.
It is known as the headquarters of the {{ill|Kōshin|en|Kōshin|WD=Q3082157|lt=Kōshin faith}} of {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province}}.
== History == In Manji 2 ({{ill|1659|en|1659|WD=Q6995|lt=1659}}), a retainer and tea master of the 2nd lord of {{ill|Koizumi Domain|en|Koizumi Domain|WD=Q1005257|lt=Koizumi Domain}}, {{ill|Sadamasa Katagiri|ja|片桐貞昌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sadamasa Katagiri|1=Katagiri Sadamasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11569883}}, established the temple, making it the wish-fulfilling temple (祈願寺) of the Koizumi family.
According to the origin legend "Sōryō-ki" written by the monk Gyokusen of {{ill|Daitoku-ji|en|Daitoku-ji|WD=Q901256|lt=Daitoku-ji}}, when Sadamasa's father, {{ill|Tadataka Katagiri|ja|片桐貞隆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tadataka Katagiri|1=Tadataka Katagiri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11569889}}, burned and discarded the family's ritual implements, a single statue of {{ill|Aomen Kongo|ja|青面金剛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aomen Kongo|1=Shōmen Kongō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11662826}} was discovered amid the ashes; this statue later became the principal image.
Based on the Educational System enacted in Meiji 5, the elementary school Shūdōkan—which had been opened by borrowing the Yamamoto Jumpo Residence on December 23 of Meiji 6—was temporarily relocated to the temple's warehouse in February of Meiji 7, and remained in use until it was moved to the Jōkō Henge residence in February of Meiji 9.
== Cultural Properties == [[File:Koizumi-koshindo Konrinin Yamatokoriyama Nara pref Japan09n.jpg|thumb|Henga]] * {{ill|Henga|ja|扁額|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Henga|1=biane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5358873}} – Donated by Kiyoshiro Yasumura and Yasaburō Morinouchi in Meiji 13 (1880); hung in the main hall.
== Access == * On the {{ill|Yamatoji Line|en|Yamatoji Line|WD=Q1151659|lt=Yamato Line}}, it is a 10-minute walk from {{ill|Yamato-Koizumi Station|en|Yamato-Koizumi Station|WD=Q856532|lt=Yamato Koizumi Station}}.
== Surroundings == * {{ill|Jikō-in|en|Jikō-in|WD=Q11494987|lt=Jikōin}} * {{ill|Matsuo-dera|ja|松尾寺 (大和郡山市)|lt=Matsuo Temple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuo-dera|1=Matsuo-dera|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11529606}}
== Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Konrin-in}} * {{ill|Kōshin-dō|ja|Kōshin-dō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin-dō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
== External Links == * [http://www.city.yamatokoriyama.nara.jp/report/machikado/002232.html I visited the Kōshin-dō of Aomen Kongo and Kukuri Saru] – Yamatokoriyama City Machikado Report 134 * [http://www.city.yamatokoriyama.nara.jp/kouhou/tektek/002810.html The Kōshin of Yamato Koizumi – A Mayor’s Walking Tour of the Castle Town] – Yamatokoriyama City Public Relations Tsunagari, Issue No.1026, June 1, Heisei 24

Based on the Educational System enacted in Meiji 5, the elementary school Shūdōkan—which had been opened by borrowing the Yamamoto Jumpo Residence on December 23 of Meiji 6—was temporarily relocated to the temple's warehouse in February of Meiji 7, and remained in use until it was moved to the Jōkō Henge residence in February of Meiji 9.

{{infobox religious building |name = 金輪院|native_name = 金輪院 |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image = [[File:Koizumi-koshindo Konrinin Yamatokoriyama Nara pref Japan01n.jpg|300px|金輪院]] |address = {{JPN}} 奈良県大和郡山市小泉町834 |位置 = |山号 = 尭然山 |宗旨 = |宗派 = 天台宗 |寺格 = |本尊 = 青面金剛 |創建年 = 万治2nd year （1659th year ） |開山 = |開基 = [[藤林宗源|藤林直良（宗源）]] |中興年 = |中興 = |正式名 = |別称 = 小泉Kōshin-dō |札所等 = |文化財 = 庭園、庫裏、表門、木造釈迦如来坐像（市指定） |公式HP = |公式HP名 = }} [[File:Koizumi-koshindo Konrinin Yamatokoriyama Nara pref Japan02n.jpg|thumb|本堂]] '''Konrin-in''' (金輪院) is a temple of {{ill|Tiantai|en|Tiantai|qid=Q668188|lt=Tendai Buddhism|1=Tiantai}} located in {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|qid=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}, {{ill|Yamatokōriyama|en|Yamatokōriyama|qid=Q118213|lt=Yamatokoriyama|1=Yamatokōriyama}}, {{ill|Koizumi Town|ja|小泉町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koizumi Town|1=Koizumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11462551}}.
It is more commonly referred to as '''Koizumi Kōshin-dō'''.
Its mountain name is Gyōnen-zan (尭然山), and its principal image is {{ill|Aomen Kongo|ja|青面金剛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aomen Kongo|1=Shōmen Kongō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11662826}}.
It is known as the headquarters of the {{ill|Kōshin|en|Kōshin|qid=Q3082157|lt=Kōshin faith|1=Kōshin}} of {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|qid=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province|1=Yamato Province}}.
== History == In Manji 2 ({{ill|1659|en|1659|qid=Q6995|lt=1659|1=1659}}), a retainer and tea master of the 2nd lord of {{ill|Koizumi Domain|en|Koizumi Domain|qid=Q1005257|lt=Koizumi Domain|1=Koizumi Domain}}, {{ill|Sadamasa Katagiri|ja|片桐貞昌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sadamasa Katagiri|1=Katagiri Sekishū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11569883}}, established the temple, making it the wish-fulfilling temple (祈願寺) of the Koizumi family.
According to the origin legend "Sōryō-ki" written by the monk Gyokusen of {{ill|Daitoku-ji|en|Daitoku-ji|qid=Q901256|lt=Daitoku-ji|1=Daitoku-ji}}, when Sadamasa's father, {{ill|Tadataka Katagiri|ja|片桐貞隆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tadataka Katagiri|1=Katagiri Sadataka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11569889}}, burned and discarded the family's ritual implements, a single statue of {{ill|Aomen Kongo|ja|青面金剛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aomen Kongo|1=Shōmen Kongō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11662826}} was discovered amid the ashes; this statue later became the principal image.
Based on the Educational System enacted in Meiji 5, the elementary school Shūdōkan—which had been opened by borrowing the Yamamoto Jumpo Residence on December 23 of Meiji 6—was temporarily relocated to the temple's warehouse in February of Meiji 7, and remained in use until it was moved to the Jōkō Henge residence in February of Meiji 9.
== Cultural Properties == [[File:Koizumi-koshindo Konrinin Yamatokoriyama Nara pref Japan09n.jpg|thumb|Henga]] * {{ill|Henga|ja|扁額|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Henga|1=biane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q5358873}} – Donated by Kiyoshiro Yasumura and Yasaburō Morinouchi in Meiji 13 (1880); hung in the main hall.
== Access == * On the {{ill|Yamatoji Line|en|Yamatoji Line|qid=Q1151659|lt=Yamato Line|1=Yamatoji Line}}, it is a 10-minute walk from {{ill|Yamato-Koizumi Station|en|Yamato-Koizumi Station|qid=Q856532|lt=Yamato Koizumi Station|1=Yamato-Koizumi Station}}.
== Surroundings == * {{ill|Jikō-in|en|Jikō-in|qid=Q11494987|lt=Jikōin|1=Jikō-in}} * {{ill|Matsuo-dera|ja|松尾寺 (大和郡山市)|lt=Matsuo Temple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuo-dera|1=Matsuo-dera Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11529606}}
== Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Konrin-in}} * {{ill|Kōshin-dō|ja|Kōshin-dō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin-dō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
== External Links == * [http://www.city.yamatokoriyama.nara.jp/report/machikado/002232.html I visited the Kōshin-dō of Aomen Kongo and Kukuri Saru] – Yamatokoriyama City Machikado Report 134 * [http://www.city.yamatokoriyama.nara.jp/kouhou/tektek/002810.html The Kōshin of Yamato Koizumi – A Mayor’s Walking Tour of the Castle Town] – Yamatokoriyama City Public Relations Tsunagari, Issue No.1026, June 1, Heisei 24
